description:
About the job

About The Company

DiDi Global Inc. is the world’s leading mobility technology platform. It offers a wide range of app-based services across markets including Asia-Pacific, Latin America and Africa, including ride hailing, taxi hailing, chauffeur, hitch and other forms of shared mobility as well as auto solutions, food delivery, intra-city freight, and financial services.

DiDi provides car owners, drivers, and delivery partners with flexible work and income opportunities. It is committed to collaborating with policymakers, the taxi industry, the automobile industry and the communities to solve the world’s transportation, environmental and employment challenges through the use of AI technology and localized smart transportation innovations. DiDi strives to create better life experiences and greater social value, by building a safe, inclusive and sustainable transportation and local services ecosystem for cities of the future.

For more information, please visit: www.didiglobal.com/news

About The Team/role

The Customer Service Coordinator focuses on delivering exceptional customer service in a challenging and fast-paced environment. The successful candidate will drive a "Customer First" mentality, foster loyalty through personalized service, and ensure operational excellence. The specialist will also play a critical role in continuous improvement efforts, identifying areas for process optimization and developing strategies to improve service, efficiency, and performance. The ideal candidate must be organized, detail-oriented, adaptable to change, and possess strong communication skills. Additionally, they must be capable of managing time efficiently while handling multiple tasks, with a problem-solving mindset that includes conducting root cause analysis and driving process optimizations.

In this role, you'll be...


Playing a key role in projects within a functional CX area.
Bringing expertise to their CX area and executing complex tasks efficiently, with limited guidance.
Suggesting process improvements and contributing to refining current playbooks.
Leading operational teams under the guidance of senior members in some circumstances.
Working directly with the BPO.
Owning full responsibility for the BPO Operation for a Fintech Line of Business.
Building strong stakeholder relationships with your LOB Supervisors.
Mentoring the BPO team.
Understanding each LOB Operation to provide insights to the Business.
Being data-driven, associating the operation with your metrics performance, and implementing process adjustments when necessary.
Contributing to the CX Ops Team to implement High Impact Projects.
Ensuring the Service Delivery role is adopted.
Generate weekly BPO status reports for your LOB.
Leading High Impact projects from conception to completion.



We're eager to be in touch because you have...


Minimum of 2-4 years of experience in service delivery in-house, operations, or a BPO environment with direct exposure to process improvement, data analysis, and incident management.
Bachelor’s Degree in a business-related field or equivalent experience.
Proficient in tools like Excel and Google Sheets.
Proven experience with BPO management tools and CRM systems.
Seeking candidates with broader business exposure, not limited to frontline contact center operations.
Fluency in English is desired, both written and spoken.



You'll love working at DiDi because...

We create user value

We strive to always create valuable experiences for our users in everything we do. Our focus is to always innovate new experiences that are safe, pleasant and efficient.

We are data-driven

We are strong believers in making informed decisions, that’s why we are data-driven. We can better navigate the business landscape strategically by analyzing valuable metrics.

Win-win Collaboration

Success is a team sport. When we work to help our partners and colleagues win, we win, too. While keeping everyone's best interest at heart, we communicate with candor and execute with excellence in all we do.

We believe in integrity

Integrity is at the very core of our business. We are people who always want to do the right thing. Our intentions are sincere, we speak our minds and listen to each other.

Growth

We always strive to do better. That means venturing beyond our comfort zones, learning from our mistakes, and helping each other grow.

Diversity and Inclusion

Diversity is one of our biggest strengths. Our differences are what make us distinct. We respect each other and believe in equal opportunities for all.

We are committed to building inclusive and diverse teams

At DiDi, we believe that our differences are our biggest source of strength. That’s why we are committed to promoting equal opportunities to all candidates and employees as an Equal Opportunity Employer.

Employment and advancement decisions at DiDi are always made based on the needs of the position and the qualifications of the candidate. We do not discriminate against any employee or applicant based on their gender, age, sexual orientation, nationality, marital status, pregnancy/maternity, disability, race, religion and beliefs, or any other status protected by applicable laws wherever we operate.

We are committed to building inclusive and diverse teams, and a workplace that is free from discrimination and harassment, because that’s how we create better products and services, make better decisions and better serve the communities we’re a part of.

title: Screening Specialist, Mexico
skills: Screening Assets - Manage comprehensive asset mastering for all tiers of Netflix screenings, collaborating with filmmakers/showrunners and Netflix Post teams to ensure 100% technical integrity, peak presentation quality, and error-free delivery across all events.
Screening Asset Distribution - Coordinating with event leads and the venue to ensure the correct content version is delivered securely, on time, and with no playback issues.
Screening Production - Executing flawless in-person and virtual screenings, including equipment setup, calibration, filmmaker/showrunner attended tech checks, and show playback.
Creation of Screening Assets including but not limited to: DCPs, Media Files (h264 and Apple ProRes), DVDs/BDs, viewing links, and more.
Work closely with Screenings and Post teams to anticipate asset needs in advance of incoming screening requests.
Familiarizing themselves with their assigned titles to anticipate potential issues (aspect ratios, forced narratives).
Familiarizing themselves with the associated title campaigns to better anticipate and prioritize timelines, budgets and workload.
Building and tracking asset delivery timelines to ensure assets are completed in advance of relevant campaign dates and incoming screening requests.
Communicate effectively with various cross-functional teams to ensure proper planning, availability and delivery of material.
Maintain, curate and expand a regional network of digital cinema mastering and distribution facilities and technical vendors.
Liaise with filmmakers to complete necessary cuts/edits for the creation of screening assets, and coordinate proper QC of the final asset.
Conduct qualitative review of the created files to ensure they are correct and meet the creation specifications.
Manage scheduling, tracking, and verification of international deliveries of screenings, festivals, and theatrical assets to venues.
Manage digital cinema audio/visual engineers at premieres and special screenings.
Troubleshooting file based delivery and creation issues in a quick and constructive manner.

This position is part of the broader Marketing organization and sits within the Events function. The ideal candidate will be able to effectively prioritize tasks and initiatives from a marketing perspective in alignment with broader marketing objectives.
This role is based in Mexico City, but the ideal candidate should have the flexibility to travel abroad if needed.

Event production- Knowledge of the event production ecosystem in Mexico, including suppliers, venues, average market prices, and local expert contacts according to the event's needs. Leadership and operational capacity to run an event from a technical standpoint.
ROS Execution - Oversight of complete in-theatre event run of show, from Q&As to musical performances or presentations.
A/V Displays & Systems - Beyond cinema projection, delivering optimal presentation on anything from TVs to large LED walls, whether in-theatre or at the event.
Lighting Design - Provide in-theatre stage and room lighting to coincide with the ambience and feel of the overall event.
A/V Capture - Coordinate with video and photo teams for in-theatre setup and lighting for recording and live-streaming.
Core technical skills & being a proactive project manager within the context of being an “Unusually Responsible Person”.
Proactivity and motivation.
The specialist needs to be strong enough in their work to support the team in achieving greater efficiencies and operational excellence.
Effective communication that can liaise between internal stakeholders and external partners.
Prioritization and working within the setting of “Context not Control”, with strong time management skills, and being able to work on multiple projects at the same time.
Being able to make informed decisions aligned with project needs
Ability to speak/write in Spanish and English is essential.
description:
About the job

Netflix is one of the world's leading entertainment services, with over 300 million paid memberships in over 190 countries enjoying TV series, films and games across a wide variety of genres and languages. Members can play, pause and resume watching as much as they want, anytime, anywhere, and can change their plans at any time.

About The Role.

The Screenings Specialist role focuses on the end-to-end work required to deliver Netflix content to off-service audiences at the best possible quality. The core of this work comprises:


Screening Assets - Manage comprehensive asset mastering for all tiers of Netflix screenings, collaborating with filmmakers/showrunners and Netflix Post teams to ensure 100% technical integrity, peak presentation quality, and error-free delivery across all events.
Screening Asset Distribution - Coordinating with event leads and the venue to ensure the correct content version is delivered securely, on time, and with no playback issues. 
Screening Production - Executing flawless in-person and virtual screenings, including equipment setup, calibration, filmmaker/showrunner attended tech checks, and show playback.


In addition, the team provides asset-heavy support for a high volume of award submissions and VOD screening links.

The Screening Specialist needs to have a strong understanding of digital cinema packages and media creation. An expertise in post-production, theatrical screenings, and distribution/operations, with experience working on promotional events where flexibility is crucial for success. They should be able to communicate technical information to both technical and non-technical audiences.

Key Responsibilities


Creation of Screening Assets including but not limited to: DCPs, Media Files (h264 and Apple ProRes), DVDs/BDs, viewing links, and more.
Work closely with Screenings and Post teams to anticipate asset needs in advance of incoming screening requests.
Familiarizing themselves with their assigned titles to anticipate potential issues (aspect ratios, forced narratives).
Familiarizing themselves with the associated title campaigns to better anticipate and prioritize timelines, budgets and workload.
Building and tracking asset delivery timelines to ensure assets are completed in advance of relevant campaign dates and incoming screening requests.
Communicate effectively with various cross-functional teams to ensure proper planning, availability and delivery of material.
Maintain, curate and expand a regional network of digital cinema mastering and distribution facilities and technical vendors.
Liaise with filmmakers to complete necessary cuts/edits for the creation of screening assets, and coordinate proper QC of the final asset.
Conduct qualitative review of the created files to ensure they are correct and meet the creation specifications.
Manage scheduling, tracking, and verification of international deliveries of screenings, festivals, and theatrical assets to venues.
Manage digital cinema audio/visual engineers at premieres and special screenings.
Troubleshooting file based delivery and creation issues in a quick and constructive manner.
This position is part of the broader Marketing organization and sits within the Events function. The ideal candidate will be able to effectively prioritize tasks and initiatives from a marketing perspective in alignment with broader marketing objectives.
This role is based in Mexico City, but the ideal candidate should have the flexibility to travel abroad if needed.


Expertise

The ideal candidate for this role should have expertise in the following topics: 


Event production- Knowledge of the event production ecosystem in Mexico, including suppliers, venues, average market prices, and local expert contacts according to the event's needs. Leadership and operational capacity to run an event from a technical standpoint.
ROS Execution - Oversight of complete in-theatre event run of show, from Q&As to musical performances or presentations.
A/V Displays & Systems - Beyond cinema projection, delivering optimal presentation on anything from TVs to large LED walls, whether in-theatre or at the event.
Lighting Design - Provide in-theatre stage and room lighting to coincide with the ambience and feel of the overall event. 
A/V Capture - Coordinate with video and photo teams for in-theatre setup and lighting for recording and live-streaming.


Skills


Core technical skills & being a proactive project manager within the context of being an “Unusually Responsible Person”.
Proactivity and motivation. 
The specialist needs to be strong enough in their work to support the team in achieving greater efficiencies and operational excellence.
Effective communication that can liaise between internal stakeholders and external partners.
Prioritization and working within the setting of “Context not Control”, with strong time management skills, and being able to work on multiple projects at the same time. 
Being able to make informed decisions aligned with project needs
Ability to speak/write in Spanish and English is essential.


Inclusion is a Netflix value and we strive to host a meaningful interview experience for all candidates. If you want an accommodation/adjustment for a disability or any other reason during the hiring process, please send a request to your recruiting partner.

We are an equal-opportunity employer and celebrate diversity, recognizing that diversity builds stronger teams. We approach diversity and inclusion seriously and thoughtfully. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, ancestry, national origin, caste, sex, sexual orientation, gender, gender identity or expression, age, disability, medical condition, pregnancy, genetic makeup, marital status, or military service.

description:
About the job

Job Title: QA Engineer (Remote)
Work Mode: Fully Remote
Schedule: Flexible (up to 40 hours per week, based on project availability)




Role Overview:

We are looking for software engineers across Latin America to join a paid remote AI training program. In this role, you will review and evaluate AI-generated code, rate AI responses for accuracy and quality, and write coding challenges to help improve AI model performance. This is a flexible, remote opportunity well-suited for software engineers seeking project-based, asynchronous work. No prior AI experience required.




Key Responsibilities

Review and evaluate code written by AI models for accuracy, efficiency, and best practices
Rate AI responses across key dimensions including correctness, instruction-following, and quality
Write original computer science questions and coding challenges to test AI capabilities
Identify bugs, errors, and inconsistencies in AI-generated code using structured guidelines
Provide clear, constructive, and structured written feedback to improve AI responses
Follow project guidelines, quality standards, and confidentiality requirements




Qualifications

Software engineering experience — junior, mid-level, or senior welcome
Proficiency in one or more of the following: Python, Java, JavaScript, TypeScript, C++, Swift, or Rust
Strong attention to detail, analytical thinking, and problem-solving skills
Background in full stack, backend, frontend, mobile, or any engineering discipline




Eligibility Requirements

Candidates must be legally authorised to work in one of the following locations:
Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Belize, Chile, Colombia, Costa Rica, Dominican Republic, Dominica, Ecuador, El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras, Mexico, Nicaragua, Panama, Paraguay, Peru, Puerto Rico, Trinidad and Tobago, Uruguay, Venezuela




Compensation

Earn up to $34 USD/hr — core project rate with additional performance incentives averaging 7.5% on top
Payments issued weekly via supported payout platforms (e.g., PayPal or AirTM)




Work Arrangement

Fully remote and asynchronous
Flexible workload depending on project availability
Free access to leading AI tools worth $200+/month included




Equal Opportunity Statement Selection decisions are based solely on skills, qualifications, and project requirements. We are committed to inclusive and fair engagement practices and consider all qualified applicants without regard to legally protected characteristics.




Ready to put your coding skills to work on AI? Apply now — interviews begin within days of application.

description:
About the job

Description

Amazon Now is looking for a MX leader for this business to drive the P&L and the strategy for the short term and long term.

Key job responsibilities

This role is responsible for managing the 3P partnership relationships where business problems, account strategy, and operational direction may not be clearly defined.

A day in the life

The role will manage the P&L, the short term and long term strategy, the relationship with the 3P, a team that overseas product management, account management, marketing, operations and growth. The role will operate with limited guidance, designing solutions and managing plans in the absence of established SOPs for a business in early growth phase. The candidate will have a strong collaboration with Amazon Now WW teams to bring best practices to MX.

About The Team

Inclusive team culture

At Amazon we embrace our differences and we are committed to furthering our culture of inclusion. We host annual and ongoing learning experiences. Amazon’s culture of inclusion is reinforced within our 14 Leadership Principles, which remind team members to seek diverse perspectives, learn and be curious, and earn trust.

Work Life Balance

We believe striking the right balance between your personal and professional life is critical to life-long happiness and fulfillment. We offer flexibility in working hours and encourage you to find your own balance between your work and personal lives.

Mentorship & Career Growth

Our team is dedicated to supporting new members. We have a broad mix of experience levels and tenures, and we’re building an environment that celebrates knowledge sharing and mentorship. We care about your career growth and strive to assign projects based on what will help each team member develop into a better-rounded professional.

Basic Qualifications


 12+ years of product or program management, product marketing, business development or technology experience
 6+ years of team management experience
 Bachelor's degree
 Experience owning/driving roadmap strategy and definition
 Experience with end to end product delivery
 Experience with feature delivery and tradeoffs of a product
 Speak, write, and read fluently in English, and have the ability to take direction in English


Preferred Qualifications


 Experience engaging and influencing senior executives


Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. skills: Campaign Localization & Co-Marketing Execution: Jointly develop demand generation initiatives and execute co-owned campaigns with partners; scale global campaigns through established localization processes and adapt content for local market execution across Brazil and Mexico.

description:
About the job

Are you an experienced, passionate pioneer in technology? A Partner Marketing Integration Senior Consultant who wants to work in a collaborative environment? As an experienced Partner Marketing Integration Senior Consultant, you will have the ability to share new ideas and collaborate on projects as a consultant without the extensive demands of travel.  Americas Delivery Mexico (ADMX) leverages scale and talent to provide high quality, cost-effective service to our clients.




ADMX is a member of the Global Delivery Network which has presence across the world with Delivery centers in the United States, Romania, India, Spain, China, and the Philippines. ADMX is in Queretaro, Mexico. We provide consulting services to help our clients achieve a higher level of service in operational efficiency and business value. We are a team of professionals passionate about serving clients with distinction and learning, and we are driven by our purpose: Making an impact that matters for our clients, our people, and society.







Work you’ll do/Responsibilities 




As a Senior Consultant, you will work with diverse global clients across a wide range of industries. You will have a variety of client facing responsibilities such as diagnosing issues using advanced analytical techniques, interviewing staff, formulating and making recommendations, and helping clients implement proposed solutions.




Campaign Localization & Co-Marketing Execution: Jointly develop demand generation initiatives and execute co-owned campaigns with partners; scale global campaigns through established localization processes and adapt content for local market execution across Brazil and Mexico.
Event Execution & Management: Support end-to-end execution of partner events, including Google-hosted and third-party activities; manage invitation and registration processes, venue coordination, supplier support, and event logistics for partner-facing initiatives.
Collaborative Swimlanes & Field Integration: Work hand-in-hand with Field Marketing in distinct but highly collaborative swimlanes to maximize impact, ensure seamless execution, and align partner programs with target account strategies and field priorities.
Brand Compliance & Content Localization: Ensure partner-facing campaigns and co-marketing assets meet Google brand standards; support localized collateral production and partner adherence to co-branding guidelines.
Lead Management & Opportunity Tracking: Manage the flow of opportunities from local partners, especially those tied to co-marketing funds; support lead upload in Vector and liaise with partner sales teams to validate follow-up and pipeline contribution.
Playbook Creation & Enablement: Create and maintain a Partner Marketing Playbook that documents best practices for co-marketing, branding, partner integration, and field collaboration to support consistent execution across markets and agencies.










The Team 




Our Deloitte Customer team empowers organizations to build deeper relationships with customers through innovative strategies, advanced analytics, Generative AI, transformative technologies, and creative design. We can enhance customer experiences and drive sustained growth and customer value creation and capture, through customer and commercial strategies, digital products and innovation, marketing, commerce, sales, and service. We are a team of strategists, data scientists, operators, creatives, designers, engineers, and architects. Our team balances business strategy, technology, creativity, and ongoing managed services to solve the biggest problems that affect customers, partners, constituents, and the workforce.




Qualifications

Required

6-10+ years of consulting and/or industry experience
Completion of course of study/Egresado in Computer Science/IT/Computer Engineering, or any pertinent field or industry, or equivalent years of experience
Responsible for supporting and leading project workstreams and/or teams
Identifies key drivers, defines problems and proposes solutions
Advanced English level
Relevant experience in partner marketing, channel marketing, field marketing, alliances, or similar B2B marketing roles
Experience developing and executing co-marketing plans, joint campaigns, and local Go-to-Market activations with partners
Practical experience supporting partner events, field integration, and joint marketing execution
Experience coordinating with partners, partner sales teams, local field teams, and external agencies
Strong project management and stakeholder coordination skills across multiple internal and external workstreams
Strong understanding of partner integration within field marketing activities and local market execution
Experience managing brand compliance, content localization, and partner-facing campaign materials

description:
About the job

Netflix is one of the world's leading entertainment services, with over 300 million paid memberships in over 190 countries enjoying TV series, films and games across a wide variety of genres and languages. Members can play, pause and resume watching as much as they want, anytime, anywhere, and can change their plans at any time.

The role 

The Production Finance Associate is a key operational partner who supports Managers and cross-functional teams in the financial execution of productions. They are responsible for tactical management, basic financial analysis, and operational coordination, ensuring consistency, control, and compliance across all production processes. They operate with a high degree of autonomy in operational tasks, demonstrating initiative, curiosity, and problem-solving ability, aligned with the team's priorities and Netflix's principles.

Core Responsibilities


Effectively coordinate with external production companies, internal teams, and stakeholders to ensure the proper financial execution of productions.
Provide close follow-up on budgets, cost reports, and variance analyses, generating clear and actionable commentary throughout the full project lifecycle.
Process and monitor funding, ensuring accurate execution and recording.
Manage and update information in internal financial systems and production tools.
Prepare key financial materials, such as budget top sheets, for approval.
Support the onboarding of external accounting teams and ensure alignment with Netflix best practices.
Conduct budget versus actual comparative analyses and identify risks or opportunities in a timely manner.
Act as an operational point of contact for internal teams such as AP, Accounting, Tax, and others.
Participate in continuous improvement initiatives, operational efficiency efforts, and the adoption of new tools.


Analytics, Technical Skills & Ways of Working


Strong foundation in accounting, finance, and production processes, with an active interest in continuing to strengthen this knowledge.
Ability to execute basic financial analysis with minimal supervision, including budget versus actuals, variance analysis, and cost tracking.
Required experience with:
Movie Magic Budgeting (MMB)
PSL or other production management systems
Knowledge of:
Tax processes and regulatory compliance in Mexico
International accounting processes
Ability to work with data analysis and automation tools:
Google Sheets at an intermediate to advanced level, including formulas, data structuring, and validations
Experience with Apps Script or workflow automation
Active adoption of technology:
Use of AI tools such as Claude, ChatGPT, or others for analysis, automation, documentation, and efficiency improvement
Ability to identify opportunities where technology can optimize operational processes
Proactive, solutions-oriented mindset, with strong attention to detail and a sense of urgency.


Communication & Collaboration


Ability to build effective working relationships with internal and external stakeholders.
Clear, structured, and direct communication, tailored to the context and audience.
Advanced English, with the ability to communicate independently with global teams, both written and verbal.
Ability to operate both collaboratively and autonomously, with a high level of organization.


Profile & Experience


Experience in Production Finance, Production Accounting, or the audiovisual industry.
Comfort working in high-demand operational environments with a large volume of information.
Highly analytical profile, with intellectual curiosity and a strong results-oriented mindset.
Ability to prioritize and execute effectively in dynamic, fast-changing environments.
Comfortable giving and receiving direct, ongoing feedback.

description:
About the job

1.) Introduction

Job Title: Hybrid Marketing & Executive Assistant
Work Hours or Schedule: Full-Time (40 hours per week, requiring significant overlap with Pacific Time business hours)
Location or Remote Status: Remote (Based in Latin America)




2.) Overview

Are you an agile professional who feels just as comfortable building a content calendar as you do organizing a chaotic executive schedule?

We are a high-performance, founder-led company operating at the intersection of media production, marketing, and business education. We help top-tier global entrepreneurs scale their brands using cutting-edge content strategies. We are looking for a unique hybrid talent: a Bilingual Marketing Operations & Executive Assistant to serve as the structural backbone and creative partner to our fast-moving director.

This role is a true 50/50 split. One day you will be organizing logistics, syncing calendars, and automating administrative workflows; the next day you will be diving into audience psychology, reviewing digital video drafts, and helping localize creative concepts. If you want a stable, high-growth remote position where your administrative execution and marketing intuition are equally valued, this position is built for you.




3.) Key Responsibilities

Administrative Partnership (50%): Own and optimize the executive’s complex calendar, anticipate scheduling friction points, coordinate logistics, and proactively clear administrative tasks before they reach the director.
Marketing & Media Support (50%): Act as the core operational bridge between the director and the internal video production teams, ensuring deliverables hit deadlines.
Content Auditing: Review raw and edited long-form or short-form video drafts to evaluate structural pacing, music alignment, and visual engagement hooks.
Creative Benchmarking: Conduct proactive research across platforms like YouTube, Instagram, and TikTok to identify trending digital frameworks and compile content briefs.
Content Localization: Analyze successful digital marketing concepts in Spanish and creatively adapt/translate them into high-performing ideas tailored to an English-speaking audience.
Process Automation: Build, document, and update Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) using workspace tools and advanced AI applications to maximize daily operational output.




4.) Must-Have Requirements

Experience: 3 to 5+ years of combined professional experience split across Executive/Administrative Support and Digital Marketing, Copywriting, or Social Media Coordination.
Language: Absolute bilingual fluency in English and Spanish with elite verbal and written communication skills in both.
Operational Drive: A deeply proactive, resource-driven personality with a proven track record of managing up and solving problems without supervision.
Digital Literacy: A sharp understanding of content marketing pipelines, audience engagement patterns, and basic video formatting structures.
Discretion: absolute compliance with non-disclosure requirements and total confidentiality when handling executive data.




5.) Nice-to-Have Requirements

A university degree in Marketing, Communications, Digital Media, or a related creative field.
Prior experience directly supporting agency founders, media executives, or online creators.
High comfort level utilizing generative AI prompting workflows and advanced productivity dashboards.




6.) Why Join Us

This is an incredible opportunity to break away from standard, boring data entry and take an active seat inside a world-class media engine. You will be treated as an equal partner in our growth, given the autonomy to control project timelines, and heavily rewarded for both your organizational mastery and creative marketing insights. If you are a remote professional in Latin America looking for a stable, fast-paced environment where no two days are the same, we want you on our team.

description:
About the job

Description

Our Finance Operations team has built a transformational platform that replaced disconnected systems and streamlined financial processes. We're expanding our Mexico-based team and looking for finance professionals with strong technical aptitude, people who understand accounting and procurement fundamentals and can leverage automation tools and AI to do the work better, faster, and at scale.

Key job responsibilities

Why This Role Matters

You'll Join a Small Mexico-based Team Providing Americas Coverage For a Platform That Manages Substantial Marketing Spend Globally. Your Work Directly Enables


 Business teams to focus on content and marketing strategy instead of administrative tasks
 Finance leadership to make data-driven decisions with real-time visibility
 Accounting to close books faster with unprecedented accuracy
 Senior leadership to trust financial data for strategic planning
 You'll be the person who understands both the finance process and the technology that powers it ensuring purchase orders flow correctly, budgets reconcile, and the systems behind it work.


What You'll Do

Support Finance Operations with Technical Expertise. You'll manage purchase order workflows, budget tracking, vendor setup, and spend reconciliation, but you'll do it with a technical lens. You understand how the systems behind these processes connect, where they break, and how to fix or improve them. You'll be the bridge between "what finance needs" and "how the platform delivers it."

A day in the life

Day-to-day, This Means


 Managing purchase orders with accuracy and speed
 Tracking budgets and providing real-time spend visibility to business teams
 Reconciling financial data across systems and flagging discrepancies
 Ensuring compliance with procurement policies and accounting standards
 Supporting month-end close activities and accrual processes
 Being the go-to person when something in the workflow isn't working right


You'll experiment with and implement AI tools to classify transactions, flag anomalies, draft communications, and assist with reconciliation.

You'll stay current on emerging AI capabilities and identify where they can reduce toil in your day-to-day work.

You'll conduct monthly business reviews with marketing and industry teams across the Americas and partner with Finance, Accounting, Legal, and Global Procurement to resolve complex issues.

What Success Looks Like

Accuracy & Compliance: Transactions processed correctly, on time, with proper controls

Responsiveness: Business teams get fast, knowledgeable support on finance queries

Automation Impact: You measurably reduce manual work through workflows you build and maintain

Technical Reliability: The automations you build are well-documented, don't break silently, and fit cohesively into the team's broader platform strategy

Collaboration: Trusted partnerships across time zones and functions

About The Team

Inclusive team culture

At Amazon we embrace our differences and we are committed to furthering our culture of inclusion. We host annual and ongoing learning experiences. Amazon’s culture of inclusion is reinforced within our 14 Leadership Principles, which remind team members to seek diverse perspectives, learn and be curious, and earn trust.

Work Life Balance

We believe striking the right balance between your personal and professional life is critical to life-long happiness and fulfillment. We offer flexibility in working hours and encourage you to find your own balance between your work and personal lives.

Mentorship & Career Growth

Our team is dedicated to supporting new members. We have a broad mix of experience levels and tenures, and we’re building an environment that celebrates knowledge sharing and mentorship. We care about your career growth and strive to assign projects based on what will help each team member develop into a better-rounded professional.

Basic Qualifications


 1+ years of finance experience
 Knowledge of standard software including Excel, Access, Oracle, Essbase, SQL and VBA skills
 Bachelor's degree or above in accounting, information systems, finance, or equivalent
 Speak, write, and read fluently in English, and have the ability to take direction in English
 Demonstrated experience building automated workflows using any automation platform (make.com, Zapier, Power Automate, or similar)


Preferred Qualifications


 Speak, write, and read fluently in Spanish
 Experience in modeling complex business scenarios
 Knowledge of accounting principles, processes, and entries
 Experience with monday.com (board configuration, automations, integrations)
 Experience with AI tools for productivity or data processing
 Fluent in Portuguese (written and verbal)


Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.


Company - Servicios Comerciales Amazon Mexico S. de R.L. de C.V.

title: Creative Strategist
skills: A clear, repeatable creative testing system running across your client portfolio with consistent velocity to new concepts, angles, and iterations

Create world-class creative briefs that transform insights into crisp, actionable direction for designers and video editors including hooks, messaging frameworks, visual concepts, format specifications, and success criteria
Direct performance storytelling by mapping the customer journey from awareness through consideration to purchase and translating it into compelling paid social concepts
Build UGC and production guidance including shot lists, video scripts, creator direction, and content planning that supports performance-oriented execution
Manage creator relationships by sourcing, coordinating, and guiding content creators to produce on-brief, performance-oriented content that aligns with testing objectives
Write high-performing ad copy including primary text, headlines, hooks, and on-screen text that aligns with strategic goals and testing frameworks
Collaborate on conversion rate optimization by partnering with teams on landing page improvements and conversion-focused messaging updates
Track and communicate performance by monitoring key performance indicators by concept and angle, synthesizing learnings, and reporting insights to internal teams and clients
Develop inspiration and competitor boards to inform creative direction and identify market trends and opportunities
Lead copy across paid ads for in-ad copy and headline variations that drive engagement and conversions
1-2 years of experience in ecommerce or performance marketing with strong preference for direct-to-consumer brand experience
Proven track record of paid social creative strategy with expertise in Meta Ads (required) and familiarity with TikTok or YouTube (preferred)
Strong grasp of creative testing methodology including hypothesis development, structured testing frameworks, iteration velocity, and performance analysis
Experience working with design and video teams from brief development through execution to iteration, with ability to provide clear, actionable creative direction
Data-fluent approach with comfort using performance metrics including click-through rates, conversion rates, cost per acquisition, and return on ad spend to make decisions and guide priorities
Deep understanding of customer psychology, marketing funnels, and storytelling principles that drive direct response performance
Excellent copywriting skills across multiple formats including hooks, scripts, ad copy, and landing pages with proven ability to write persuasive, conversion-focused content
Highly organized with strong attention to detail, quality control processes, and ability to manage multiple clients and deadlines simultaneously
Proficiency with Meta Ads Manager, Shopify, Google Analytics, Figma, Motion, and collaboration tools including Slack, Asana, or Notion
Client-facing experience including presenting strategy, leading client calls, and confidently pushing for action based on data and creative insights
Experience with conversion rate optimization and landing page testing frameworks to improve post-click performance
Experience building creative playbooks, training materials, or onboarding systems for creative teams
Familiarity with additional paid social platforms including TikTok Ads, YouTube Ads, or Pinterest Ads
Background in agency environments managing multiple client accounts with varying needs and timelines
Generous Time Off: Competitive PTO policy and paid holidays to maintain work-life balance
Fully Remote: Work from anywhere on US hours and a distributed team culture
Compensation: Competitive salary commensurate with experience.
description:
About the job

About Lilo Social

Lilo Social is a full-funnel eCommerce growth agency specializing in acquisition and retention for direct-to-consumer brands. With a team of over 75 professionals, we deliver best-in-class paid media, email marketing, and creative services to help brands scale profitably. We work across the entire customer lifecycle: from content creation and paid advertising to conversion rate optimization and retention marketing.




Our partnership-first approach has earned us a 90% client retention rate and recognition as an Inc. 5000 honoree for three consecutive years (2023, 2024, 2025). We are proud to be a Klaviyo Master Elite Agency, a distinction held by only 0.2% of Klaviyo's 6,500+ partners. To date, we have driven billions of dollars in attributed revenue for our partners.




The Role

Lilo Social is seeking a Creative Strategist to lead performance-driven creative strategy across a portfolio of direct-to-consumer brands. You will own the end-to-end creative testing engine—from research and messaging frameworks to briefs, storyboards, conversion rate optimization ideas, and ongoing iteration—helping clients consistently find creative winners and scale spend profitably. This is a highly cross-functional role where you will partner closely with design, video, media buying, and account teams while working directly with clients to set creative direction, align on priorities, and build repeatable roadmaps for growth.




What Success Looks Like

A clear, repeatable creative testing system running across your client portfolio with consistent velocity to new concepts, angles, and iterations
Creative insights that translate into measurable performance improvements including higher click-through rates, conversion rates, lower customer acquisition costs, and scalable winning campaigns
Strong client trust in your strategic point of view, demonstrated through proactive communication, data-backed recommendations, and leadership in creative direction
Elevated internal creative standards through contributions to team training, standard operating procedures, and systems that improve output quality and speed




Responsibilities

Lead research and insights by mining customer reviews, competitor ads libraries, audience pain points, and product differentiators to identify winning angles and positioning strategies

Build UGC and production guidance including shot lists, video scripts, creator direction, and content planning that supports performance-oriented execution
Manage creator relationships by sourcing, coordinating, and guiding content creators to produce on-brief, performance-oriented content that aligns with testing objectives
Write high-performing ad copy including primary text, headlines, hooks, and on-screen text that aligns with strategic goals and testing frameworks
Collaborate on conversion rate optimization by partnering with teams on landing page improvements and conversion-focused messaging updates
Track and communicate performance by monitoring key performance indicators by concept and angle, synthesizing learnings, and reporting insights to internal teams and clients
Develop inspiration and competitor boards to inform creative direction and identify market trends and opportunities
Lead copy across paid ads for in-ad copy and headline variations that drive engagement and conversions




Requirements

1-2 years of experience in ecommerce or performance marketing with strong preference for direct-to-consumer brand experience
Proven track record of paid social creative strategy with expertise in Meta Ads (required) and familiarity with TikTok or YouTube (preferred)
Strong grasp of creative testing methodology including hypothesis development, structured testing frameworks, iteration velocity, and performance analysis
Experience working with design and video teams from brief development through execution to iteration, with ability to provide clear, actionable creative direction
Data-fluent approach with comfort using performance metrics including click-through rates, conversion rates, cost per acquisition, and return on ad spend to make decisions and guide priorities
Deep understanding of customer psychology, marketing funnels, and storytelling principles that drive direct response performance
Excellent copywriting skills across multiple formats including hooks, scripts, ad copy, and landing pages with proven ability to write persuasive, conversion-focused content
Highly organized with strong attention to detail, quality control processes, and ability to manage multiple clients and deadlines simultaneously
Proficiency with Meta Ads Manager, Shopify, Google Analytics, Figma, Motion, and collaboration tools including Slack, Asana, or Notion




Nice to Have

Client-facing experience including presenting strategy, leading client calls, and confidently pushing for action based on data and creative insights
Experience with conversion rate optimization and landing page testing frameworks to improve post-click performance
Experience building creative playbooks, training materials, or onboarding systems for creative teams
Familiarity with additional paid social platforms including TikTok Ads, YouTube Ads, or Pinterest Ads

description:
About the job

To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.

Job Category

Marketing & Communications

Job Details

About Salesforce

Salesforce is the #1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all.

Ready to level-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce.

We are seeking a highly motivated, results-oriented, and experienced Marketing Strategy and Planning Manager to serve as the financial and operational backbone for the Brand Campaigns and Partnerships team. This role is central to driving operational excellence by ensuring rigorous budget management, accurate target setting, and effective annual performance pacing for high-impact brand initiatives.

Responsibilities

Budget Management & Financial Oversight:


Manage the overall Brand Campaigns and Partnerships budget, including tracking multi-year commitments and ensuring alignment with Finance.
Partner with Finance on budget reconciliations, requarterization, and scenario planning to maintain financial accuracy and flexibility.
Support the Brand and Partnerships team with quick turn and executive approvals. Drive rigor around budget submissions and invoicing deadlines.


Annual Targets & Performance Pacing


Lead the team's strategic planning framework process (V2MOM creation or similar), driving the definition and documentation of annual targets.
Own the monthly status updates and performance pacing process to ensure team targets are accurately tracked and on schedule.


Timeline & Leadership Updates


Maintain budget and milestone alignment with all Brand Campaigns and Partnerships work and campaign/media delivery timelines.
Provide bi-weekly executive-level updates with key dependencies, risks, and critical operational callouts to leadership.


Reporting & Strategic Business Operations


Provide consolidated operational reports for leadership, including comprehensive views of budget status, target pacing, and resource commitments.
Serve as the strategic business operations focus, ensuring budgets, targets, and pacing are accurate, well-communicated, and fully aligned with both leadership and Finance partners.


AI Tooling & Intelligent Operations


Serve as the team's primary technical resource for internal AI tooling, agent building, and prompt engineering.
Design, build, and iterate on AI agents and automated workflows that streamline repetitive operational tasks, improve team efficiency, and reduce manual lift across budget tracking, reporting, and cross-functional coordination.
Partner with team members to identify high-impact opportunities where AI tools can accelerate work, and translate those opportunities into practical, deployed solutions.
Develop and maintain a library of reusable prompts, tools, and AI-powered workflows tailored to the team's operational needs.


Experience / Skills Required


5+ years of progressive experience in marketing operations, finance, analytics, or a similar business operations role, preferably within a fast-paced environment.
Proven expertise in budget management, financial tracking, and operational reporting.
Advanced Excel skills required; demonstrated ability to handle large datasets with a detail-oriented approach.
Demonstrated success in driving operational processes from conception to completion, with a strong focus on measurable business outcomes.
Strong verbal and written communication skills, with the ability to articulate complex financial and operational information clearly and concisely to diverse stakeholders.
Highly analytical and data-driven, with the ability to translate insights into actionable operational strategies.
Proactive in identifying potential risks or dependencies and developing creative solutions in a dynamic environment.
Hands-on experience building AI agents, custom GPTs, or LLM-powered tools using platforms such as ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, Agentforce, or similar.
Proficiency in prompt engineering, with the ability to design effective prompts for a range of operational use cases.
Familiarity with AI workflow and automation tools (e.g., Slack AI) to build scalable, team-facing solutions.
Comfortable working at the intersection of technology and operations — able to independently scope, build, and deploy lightweight AI-powered tools without deep engineering support.


Preferred Qualifications


Familiarity with marketing campaign delivery timelines.
Experience working closely with Finance teams on budget forecasting and reconciliation.
Experience using or building AI agents within enterprise platforms (e.g., Salesforce Agentforce)
Prior experience acting as an AI champion or internal resource for a non-technical team, democratizing access to AI tools and capabilities.


Unleash Your Potential

When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and be your best, and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can do your best. Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world.

Accommodations

If you need a reasonable accommodation during the application or the recruiting process, please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form.

Please note that Salesforce uses artificial intelligence (AI) tools to help our recruiters assess and evaluate candidates’ resumes and qualifications throughout the recruiting process. Humans will always make any candidate selection and hiring decisions. Please see our Candidate Privacy Statement for more information about how we use your personal data and your rights, including with regard to use of AI tools and opt out options.

Posting Statement

Salesforce is an equal opportunity employer and maintains a policy of non-discrimination with all employees and applicants for employment. What does that mean exactly? It means that at Salesforce, we believe in equality for all. And we believe we can lead the path to equality in part by creating a workplace that’s inclusive, and free from discrimination. Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal. Any employee or potential employee will be assessed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications – without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, transgender status, age, disability, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other classifications protected by law. This policy applies to current and prospective employees, no matter where they are in their Salesforce employment journey. It also applies to recruiting, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, assessment of job performance, discipline, termination, and everything in between. Recruiting, hiring, and promotion decisions at Salesforce are fair and based on merit. The same goes for compensation, benefits, promotions, transfers, reduction in workforce, recall, training, and education.

description:
About the job

¿Quiénes somos?

En VEMO, somos una empresa de electromovilidad impulsada por su gente y tecnología, que integra todo el ecosistema de movilidad limpia para México y América Latina. Nuestra misión es acelerar la adopción de soluciones de movilidad limpia, mejorando la calidad de vida urbana, preservando el medio ambiente y generando empleo sostenible de alto impacto. Estamos comprometidos con una movilidad responsable, limpia e inteligente.




Propósito del rol

El VCN Data Analyst será responsable de centralizar, analizar y traducir los datos comerciales, operativos y de portafolio de la red de recarga VCN en inteligencia accionable que impulse la toma de decisiones estratégicas, la eficiencia operativa y la escalabilidad de capacidades internas. Este rol trabaja de forma transversal con líderes de área y en estrecha colaboración con los equipos de Business Intelligence, Business Development y VCN Operations para diseñar dashboards, reportes y herramientas que fortalezcan el seguimiento de indicadores clave de la red.




Requisitos

Licenciatura en Ingeniería, Actuaría, Economía, Matemáticas Aplicadas, Sistemas Computacionales o afines
Deseable: Maestría o posgrado en Data Science, Business Analytics o Business Intelligence
5+ años en análisis de datos, business analytics o roles similares, idealmente en industrias de tecnología, movilidad, energía o infraestructura
Experiencia en forecasting, modelos de predicción y optimización de operaciones
Historial comprobable en diseño de dashboards y automatización de reportes
Experiencia trabajando con stakeholders de alto nivel y generando recomendaciones estratégicas




Habilidades técnicas (Hard Skills)

Dominio avanzado de Excel y PowerPoint
Manejo de herramientas de visualización: Power BI, Tableau
Conocimientos en SQL, Python o R para análisis y modelado de datos
Sólida comprensión de métricas financieras y operativas
Experiencia en data cleaning, estructuración de bases de datos y metodologías de análisis




Habilidades interpersonales (Soft Skills)

Capacidad analítica y pensamiento estratégico
Estructura y atención al detalle
Autonomía y ownership mentality
Comunicación efectiva y data storytelling
Adaptabilidad y colaboración transversal




Nice to Have

Conocimientos en modelos de pricing y customer segmentation
Experiencia previa en empresas de alto crecimiento o scale-ups




Lo que ofrecemos

Ser parte de una empresa pionera en el ecosistema de movilidad limpia en Latinoamérica
Exposición directa a equipos directivos y senior stakeholders
Oportunidad de impactar en decisiones estratégicas y en el crecimiento de la red de recarga más importante del país
Entorno dinámico, innovador y colaborativo

title: Customer Success Renewals Specialist – Support Contracts (North America)

You will engage customers and channel partners ahead of contract expiration to create seamless renewal outcomes. Sending notifications of renewal expiration to customers.
You will communicate contract coverage, renewal options, timelines, and the value of continued support services.
You will ensure renewals, working toward renewal rate, churn, and forecasting Indicators set by leadership.

description:
About the job

Rockwell Automation is a global technology leader focused on helping the world’s manufacturers be more productive, sustainable, and agile. With more than 28,000 employees who make the world better every day, we know we have something special. Behind our customers - amazing companies that help feed the world, provide life-saving medicine on a global scale, and focus on clean water and green mobility - our people are energized problem solvers that take pride in how the work we do changes the world for the better.

We welcome all makers, forward thinkers, and problem solvers who are looking for a place to do their best work. And if that’s you we would love to have you join us!

Job Description

As Renewals Specialist you will manage a focused portfolio of customer accounts to ensure the renewal of support contracts. You will create high renewal rates by engaging customers and channel partners, communicating contract value, and removing friction from the renewal process.

As Renewals Specialist you will work through remote engagement (telephone, email, Gainsight, and CRM workflows) and partners with Customer Success, Sales, Channel, and Operations and Support teams. You will use Salesforce data and processes to manage renewals, forecast risk, and support we are recurring revenue and retention goals for North America–based customers

Your Responsibilities


You will manage a defined portfolio of accounts with a primary focus on renewing support contracts on time and in full.
You will engage customers and channel partners ahead of contract expiration to create seamless renewal outcomes. Sending notifications of renewal expiration to customers.
You will communicate contract coverage, renewal options, timelines, and the value of continued support services.
You will ensure renewals, working toward renewal rate, churn, and forecasting Indicators set by leadership.
You will use Salesforce to manage renewal opportunities, track progress, maintain accurate contract data, receive and send customer quotes, and support forecasting activities.
You will partner with Partner Success Managers to align renewal strategies with channel partners.
You will collaborate with Sales, Channel teams, Contracts, and Operations on pricing, terms, documentation, and renewal execution.
You will provide first-line support for renewal-related inquiries, including contract scope, pricing questions, and renewal logistics.
You will identify renewal risk early and escalate to ensure leadership visibility and proactive mitigation.
You will follow established Salesforce and renewal processes to ensure data quality, compliance, and operational efficiency.
You will support our revenue retention and growth strategy by reducing renewal friction and improving the customer experience.


The Essentials - You Will Have


Bachelor's degree.


The Preferred - You Might Also Have


Minimum 1–2 years of experience in Renewals, Customer Success, Inside Sales, Sales Operations, or Contract Management (preferred).
Experience working with CRM tools such as Salesforce for opportunity management, forecasting, and reporting.
Experience supporting North America–based customers.


What We Offer

Our benefits package includes …


Comprehensive mindfulness programs with a premium membership to Calm
Volunteer Paid Time off available after 6 months of employment for eligible employees.
Company volunteer and donation matching program – Your volunteer hours or personal cash donations to an eligible charity can be matched with a charitable donation.
Employee Assistance Program
Personalized wellbeing programs through our OnTrack program
On-demand digital course library for professional development... and other local benefits!


Rockwell Automation’s hybrid policy aligns that employees are expected to work at a Rockwell location at least Mondays, Tuesdays, and Thursdays unless they have a business obligation out of the office.

skills: Address inbound customer inquiries regarding our platform’s features Answer all inbound support emails within 60 minutes or less, continually striving for faster response times Triage incoming customer support emails to specialized CS team members as needed Participate in live chat during our standard business hours Schedule live customer screen-sharing sessions and phone calls as needed Investigate and troubleshoot complex customer questions using intuitive problem-solving skills Identify patterns in incoming support emails and chats, and keep an eye out for potential issues Escalate product issues to our Technical Support Specialists Contribute to our ambitious conversion rate goals by conducting live onboarding calls with customers in a free trial

Maintain alignment with the larger Customer Success team and other internal stakeholders Identify and report knowledge gaps and work to get them added to our knowledge systems Work with other SimplePractice teams to handle customer inquiries when inter-department collaboration is needed
English proficiency

description:
About the job



About Us 



SimplePractice is headquartered in Los Angeles, California, but we have team members who work and live across the United States, Dominican Republic, Mexico City, and Ukraine.



At SimplePractice, we are improving access to quality care by equipping health and wellness clinicians with all the tools they need to thrive in private practice. 



More than 250,000 providers trust SimplePractice to build their business through our industry-leading software with powerful tools that simplify every part of practice management. From admin work to clinical care, our suite of innovative solutions work together to reduce administrative burden—empowering solo and small group practitioners to thrive alongside their clients. 



Award-winning and people-first, SimplePractice is shaping the future of health tech. Recognized by MedTech Breakthrough, the Digital Health Awards, Best Places to Code in Mexico and Built In's Best Places to Work.



 



The Role



SimplePractice is seeking a friendly, eager-to-learn Customer Success Specialist (Product Specialist) to join our Customer Success team in Mexico City. 



SimplePractice is built for our customers, and supporting their success is our top priority. We strive to provide unparalleled support and encourage our customers to always let us know how we can improve our products. 



In this role, you’ll use your passion for collaboration and technology to provide our customers with a world-class experience. You would act as the front line of communication with our customers, serving inbound phone calls as well as assisting customers via chat and email. This role will give you the opportunity to provide tangible value and support to our growing community.



This is a full-time hourly (non-exempt) position, with overtime available during peak seasons. We’re looking for some individuals that can also offer non-traditional business hours availability, including weekends and evening hours, to help us manage our response time goals throughout the entire week. 



 



Responsibilities



Address inbound customer inquiries regarding our platform’s features Answer all inbound support emails within 60 minutes or less, continually striving for faster response times Triage incoming customer support emails to specialized CS team members as needed Participate in live chat during our standard business hours Schedule live customer screen-sharing sessions and phone calls as needed Investigate and troubleshoot complex customer questions using intuitive problem-solving skills Identify patterns in incoming support emails and chats, and keep an eye out for potential issues Escalate product issues to our Technical Support Specialists Contribute to our ambitious conversion rate goals by conducting live onboarding calls with customers in a free trial
Maintain alignment with the larger Customer Success team and other internal stakeholders Identify and report knowledge gaps and work to get them added to our knowledge systems Work with other SimplePractice teams to handle customer inquiries when inter-department collaboration is needed

 



Desired Skills & Experience



English proficiency 
1-2 years in a customer support or service industry role, serving a diverse and professional customer base 
You have a bachelor’s degree from an accredited college or university
You have demonstrated the ability to self-manage towards a set of defined goals & prioritize among many different tasks
A sense of urgency to complete work efficiently and accurately
Comfortable with ambiguity to determine the best solution for our customers 
Comfortable with simultaneously managing and troubleshooting a large number of computer-based programs, applications, and toolsets to perform job duties (e.g., Slack, Asana, Zendesk, Google Workspace)

 



Bonus Points



You're highly empathetic, patient, and really love helping people
You have excellent written and verbal communication skills, with an emphasis on a casual, friendly, and conversational tone
You're self-managing and self-motivating, can organize in a multitasking environment, and can adjust priorities as needed
A plus if you have past experience working with Zendesk or in SAAS companies

 



Monthly Compensation Range



MXN $20,158.33 - $25,350.00 per month 



Base salary is one component of total compensation. 



The above represents the expected base compensation range for this job requisition. Ultimately, in determining your pay, we’ll consider many factors including, but not limited to, skills, experience, qualifications, geographic location, and other job-related factors.

 

Base salary is one component of total compensation. Employees may also be eligible for an annual bonus or commission. Some roles may also be eligible for overtime pay.The amount below represents the expected annual base compensation range for this job requisition. Ultimately, in determining your pay, we’ll consider many factors including, but not limited to, skills, experience, qualifications, geographic location, and other job-related factors.



Base Compensation Range: $241,900 MXN - $302,400 MXN

description:
About the job

Customer Relationship Management Specialist




Location: Remote – Latin America

Schedule: Part-Time (20+ hours/week) with strong full-time growth potential




Overview

Are you passionate about building systems, optimizing operations, and making a meaningful impact? This is an opportunity to join a mission-driven nonprofit organization at a pivotal stage of growth.




As the CRM & Operations Manager, you'll lead the transformation of the organization's donor CRM while serving as a strategic operational partner to executive leadership. You'll help build scalable processes, improve data integrity, coordinate key initiatives, and support the launch of a major community-focused agricultural project.




Key Responsibilities

Audit, clean, and optimize donor data across CRM platforms and spreadsheets.
Build automated workflows, segmentation, and reporting to improve donor engagement.
Create dashboards and analytics to track donor retention and campaign performance.
Manage executive calendars, scheduling, and administrative operations.
Coordinate communication across executives, board members, community partners, and project stakeholders.
Support operational planning, project tracking, and AI-powered data collection initiatives.
Prepare donor communications, newsletters, reports, and fundraising materials.
Maintain documentation, meeting notes, and operational workflows as priorities evolve.




Success in this Role

You'll create reliable systems, improve operational efficiency, and become a trusted partner to leadership during a period of rapid organizational growth.




Requirements

3+ years of experience in CRM, operations, project coordination, or similar roles.
Hands-on experience with CRM platforms such as HubSpot, Salesforce, or equivalent.
Advanced Excel and/or Google Sheets skills.
Experience building reports, managing data, and improving workflows.
Fluent English and Spanish, both written and spoken.
Comfortable using AI tools and quickly learning new software.
Strong organizational skills with exceptional attention to detail.
Experience working independently in a remote environment.




What We're Looking For

Highly organized and detail-oriented.
Proactive and self-directed.
Collaborative and transparent in communication.
Adaptable in fast-paced environments.
Accountable with a strong sense of ownership.
Passionate about mission-driven work and community impact.




Nice to Have

Experience working in nonprofits, charities, or mission-driven organizations.
Background in donor relations, fundraising operations, or grant reporting.
Experience supporting executive leadership or Chief of Staff functions.
Familiarity with shared leadership or flat organizational structures.
Experience coordinating multiple stakeholders and cross-functional projects.




Why Join

Help build the operational foundation of a high-impact organization while taking ownership of systems, collaborating directly with leadership, and growing into an increasingly strategic role as the organization scales.

description:
About the job

Job Title: Customer Success Operations Coordinator
Schedule: Part-Time (20 hours/week initially), with the potential to expand based on performance and business needs
Location: Remote (Latin America Preferred)




Overview

Enjoy building strong client relationships while keeping operations running smoothly behind the scenes?




Join a growing healthcare cybersecurity company dedicated to helping healthcare organizations strengthen their security posture, protect sensitive data, and navigate today's evolving cybersecurity landscape. As the company continues to expand, they are seeking a Customer Success Operations Coordinator to become the operational backbone of their client success function.




In this role, you'll serve as the primary communication bridge between clients and internal technical teams, ensuring seamless coordination, exceptional client experiences, and long-term relationship success. This opportunity is ideal for a highly organized, detail-oriented professional who enjoys managing multiple stakeholders, communicating with confidence, and keeping projects moving forward. While deep cybersecurity expertise isn't required, an understanding of IT terminology and the ability to communicate technical concepts in client-friendly language will set you up for success.




Key Responsibilities

Organize, schedule, and coordinate Quarterly Business Review (QBR) calls with clients
Ensure meeting participants are prepared and supporting materials are delivered in advance
Serve as the primary point of contact for existing clients, responding to inquiries and coordinating internal follow-up
Facilitate communication between clients and internal technical teams to ensure timely issue resolution
Coordinate crisis communications during security incidents through phone, email, and text
Engage with clients during quarterly reviews to understand their needs and identify opportunities for service expansion and retention
Create, update, and repurpose presentations, pitch decks, and supporting materials for client meetings
Maintain accurate records of client interactions, meeting notes, and follow-up activities
Coordinate communication across internal stakeholders using Microsoft Teams, email, phone, and messaging platforms
Ensure all client communications are professional, timely, and aligned with company standards
Work with the CFO to prepare and send proposals for existing and prospective clients
Maintain accurate client activities, contacts, and records within HubSpot

Must-Have Requirements

3+ years of experience in customer success, operations coordination, account management, executive support, or a similar client-facing role
Exceptional organizational skills with strong attention to detail
Excellent written and verbal English communication skills
Strong client relationship management and communication abilities
Proficiency with Microsoft Office Suite (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook)
Experience with or the ability to quickly learn HubSpot CRM, DotLoop, or similar electronic signature solutions
Experience creating professional presentations, reports, and business documents
Ability to manage multiple priorities while coordinating across teams and stakeholders
Comfortable communicating with clients via phone, email, text, and virtual meetings
Ability to work independently while maintaining accuracy and professionalism
Reliable internet connection and professional remote work environment

Nice-to-Have Requirements

Familiarity with IT terminology and technology-related environments
Experience using HubSpot CRM
Experience using DotLoop or similar electronic signature platforms
Experience working within the healthcare or cybersecurity industries




Why Join Us

This is an opportunity to become a key member of a growing healthcare cybersecurity company where your work will directly contribute to delivering an outstanding client experience. You'll serve as the central connection between clients and internal teams, helping ensure every interaction is organized, responsive, and professional while supporting meaningful, long-term client relationships.




As the company continues to grow, you'll have the opportunity to expand your responsibilities, collaborate closely with leadership, and make a visible impact on customer success operations. If you enjoy building relationships, coordinating moving pieces, and creating exceptional client experiences in a fast-paced environment, this role offers the chance to grow your career while contributing to a mission-driven organization dedicated to protecting healthcare organizations and the communities they serve.
