description:
About the job

Rockwell Automation is a global technology leader focused on helping the world’s manufacturers be more productive, sustainable, and agile. With more than 28,000 employees who make the world better every day, we know we have something special. Behind our customers - amazing companies that help feed the world, provide life-saving medicine on a global scale, and focus on clean water and green mobility - our people are energized problem solvers that take pride in how the work we do changes the world for the better.

We welcome all makers, forward thinkers, and problem solvers who are looking for a place to do their best work. And if that’s you we would love to have you join us!

Job Description

As Renewals Specialist you will manage a focused portfolio of customer accounts to ensure the renewal of support contracts. You will create high renewal rates by engaging customers and channel partners, communicating contract value, and removing friction from the renewal process.

As Renewals Specialist you will work through remote engagement (telephone, email, Gainsight, and CRM workflows) and partners with Customer Success, Sales, Channel, and Operations and Support teams. You will use Salesforce data and processes to manage renewals, forecast risk, and support we are recurring revenue and retention goals for North America–based customers.

Your Responsibilities


You will manage a defined portfolio of accounts with a primary focus on renewing support contracts on time and in full.
You will engage customers and channel partners ahead of contract expiration to create seamless renewal outcomes. Sending notifications of renewal expiration to customers.
You will communicate contract coverage, renewal options, timelines, and the value of continued support services.
You will ensure renewals, working toward renewal rate, churn, and forecasting Indicators set by leadership.
You will use Salesforce to manage renewal opportunities, track progress, maintain accurate contract data, receive and send customer quotes, and support forecasting activities.
You will partner with Partner Success Managers to align renewal strategies with channel partners.
You will collaborate with Sales, Channel teams, Contracts, and Operations on pricing, terms, documentation, and renewal execution.
You will provide first-line support for renewal-related inquiries, including contract scope, pricing questions, and renewal logistics.
You will identify renewal risk early and escalate to ensure leadership visibility and proactive mitigation.
You will follow established Salesforce and renewal processes to ensure data quality, compliance, and operational efficiency.
You will support our revenue retention and growth strategy by reducing renewal friction and improving the customer experience.


The Essentials - You Will Have


Bachelor's degree.


The Preferred - You Might Also Have


Minimum 1–2 years of experience in Renewals, Customer Success, Inside Sales, Sales Operations, or Contract Management (preferred).
Experience working with CRM tools such as Salesforce for opportunity management, forecasting, and reporting.
Experience supporting North America–based customers.


What We Offer

Our benefits package includes …


Comprehensive mindfulness programs with a premium membership to Calm
Volunteer Paid Time off available after 6 months of employment for eligible employees.
Company volunteer and donation matching program – Your volunteer hours or personal cash donations to an eligible charity can be matched with a charitable donation.
Employee Assistance Program
Personalized wellbeing programs through our OnTrack program
On-demand digital course library for professional development... and other local benefits!


Rockwell Automation’s hybrid policy aligns that employees are expected to work at a Rockwell location at least Mondays, Tuesdays, and Thursdays unless they have a business obligation out of the office.

description:
About the job

Customer Support Specialist 




Location: Colombia / Mexico (Remote) 

Position Type: Full-Time 

Vacancy Status: This posting is for an existing vacancy.




About Us

Voices is the trusted voice partner for brands, tech and voice talent. Voices helps scale real voice solutions, across traditional voiceover, Voice AI, and ethically sourced voice data. For more than two decades, the biggest and most beloved brands have trusted Voices to find their voices and bring their projects to life.

Why join us? We’re on a mission to shape the future of voice, and we’re building a team that’s curious, collaborative, and passionate about making great ideas heard.




About the Role 

As a Customer Support Specialist within our Delivery & Operations department, you will be on the front lines providing a stellar, high-touch experience to our diverse community, from Voice Over Talent to Enterprise clients. This role is unique because it blends proactive customer problem-solving with platform integrity; you'll not only resolve inquiries but also act as a platform guardian by reviewing jobs and investigating suspicious activity to ensure trust and safety. Working in a fast-paced environment, you'll leverage tools like Salesforce, Slack, and AI-powered support tech to tag cases, log bugs, and provide front-line insights that shape our product. You will report directly to the Manager, Customer Support.




Responsibilities 

In this role, you’ll take ownership of the following key areas:

Triage and Resolve: Prioritize and answer urgent cases, calls, and emails across multiple channels. Solve immediate customer challenges while proactively heading off future problems.
Trust and Safety: Review jobs and packages to ensure legitimate postings are approved. Investigate suspicious users and execute steps to remove fraudulent accounts from the platform.
Data and Insights: Tag 100% of your assigned cases with accurate dispositions, types, and sentiment to provide data-driven insights. Actively log bugs for the IT team.
Cross-Functional Collaboration: Serve as a subject matter expert and educational resource. Attend interdepartmental meetings to share front-line insights regarding core customer challenges.
Process Improvement: Embrace available tools and technology to recommend and execute efficiency improvements in interdepartmental Customer Support workflows.




What You Bring

We’re looking for someone who brings some or all of the following:

Experience & Education: A minimum of a College diploma in Marketing, Communications, Business Administration (or equivalent), plus demonstrated experience in an advanced Customer Support role or related field.
Language Skills: Must be fluent in English. Additionally, high proficiency or native-level skills in Spanish is required.
Technical Proficiency: Familiarity with Salesforce, Slack, and Google Suite. Experience with AI-powered support tools (including chatbots) and productivity platforms is a strong asset.
Exceptional Soft Skills: Top-tier time management, agility, and the ability to juggle competing priorities with grace in a dynamic, fast-paced environment.
Customer-First Mindset: A passion for delivering high-touch customer service by listening, empathizing, and anticipating needs. You excel at analyzing complex issues and collaboratively developing innovative solutions.




Perks & Benefits

In addition to competitive compensation, we offer a range of perks that support your growth, wellbeing, and day-to-day flexibility. Here’s what you can look forward to: 

Generous Vacation
Comprehensive Health Coverage
Performance Bonus Program
Learning & Development Support
A Great Team




Remote Work Details: We champion workplace flexibility. This role is remote, but we still highly value in-person connection. You are always welcome to work from our Ontario hub, though it is never required. Should we ever need you to attend a specific event, we will fully cover your travel and lodging expenses.

 

Compensation: Competitive salary commensurate with location & experience.




Additional Notes

All interviewed candidates will receive notification within 45 days of final interviews.
Our Use of AI in Recruitment: We use an AI tool in our applicant tracking system (Jobvite) to check how well a candidate’s experience aligns with the job requirements. This feature, called Talent Match, may label an application as a match or leave it unlabelled. It does not make decisions or reject candidates. Every application is reviewed by our People Operations team.




Our Commitment to Equity & Accessibility

Voices is an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering an inclusive and accessible workplace. We welcome applications from individuals of all backgrounds and experiences.

If you require accommodation during the recruitment process, please let us know, and we will work with you to meet your needs.

 




Apply Online

If this sounds like a role where you’d thrive, we’d love to hear from you. Interested candidates are encouraged to send their cover letter and résumé by completing the online application. By doing so, your resume is guaranteed to be reviewed.




#LI-Remote AND #LI-NC1

Location: USA & Canada

Level: IC3 or IC4

Hi there!

Zapier’s mission is to make automation and AI accessible to everyone. Our enterprise customers are adopting automation as a strategic capability, not just a tool. They need expert partners who can help them orchestrate processes, unlock new use cases, and scale their impact.

We’re looking for an Automation Strategist to join our Customer Success organization as the core role working with customers on our enterprise plan. This role blends strategic advisory, orchestration design, and value-focused relationship expansion. As an Automation Strategist, you will help customers shape their automation strategy, identify and deploy high-value use cases, and guide them toward meaningful AI-enabled transformation.

This role moves beyond classic Customer Success, focusing on consulting, systems thinking, orchestration, executive alignment and continuous value discovery, rooted in our team’s mission of delivering value through clarity, impact, curiosity, and approachable expertise.


Our Commitment to Applicants
Culture and Values at Zapier
Zapier Guide to Remote Work
Zapier Code of Conduct
Diversity and Inclusivity at Zapier


About You


You see opportunity in ambiguity and building things that scale
You experiment, learn quickly, and iterate openly, unafraid to make, and revise, bets.
You enjoy creating playbooks as much as running them.
You appreciate that each customer is unique, yet you build frameworks that scale across them.
You use AI in your work today — not occasionally, but as part of how you operate at a high level. You can point to workflows you’ve built, how your approach has evolved through iteration, and the impact on quality, efficiency, and experience — while intentionally applying AI for the right outcomes, setting a high bar for outputs, and taking ownership of what ships.
You’re a strategic, customer-centric problem solver.
You’re relentlessly focused on the outcome and willing to learn new approaches or challenge the status quo to deliver value for our enterprise customers.
With 5+ years in SaaS (customer success, consulting, solutions, enterprise product or similar process and system strategy roles), customers see you as a valuable extension of their team
You have thoughts on how modern businesses should operate, including where inefficiencies hide and how automation + AI unlock leverage.
You can operate across audiences, from executive sponsors to execution teams running sprint-style projects.
You connect business goals to product solutions
You’re skilled at discovering what customers actually need (not just what they say),
You communicate automation value in terms of ROI, efficiency, scalability, and risk reduction
You think in systems, logic, and orchestration frameworks, but don’t get bogged down in technical details.
You own the outcome, project managing stakeholders internally (TAM, product, support) and externally (customer execution teams, Line of Business Leaders) to deliver realized, quantifiable value.


You’re an exceptional communicator
You communicate strategy and insights with clarity, empathy, and precision.
You know when to use async vs. live to balance ideal outcomes and speed, employing Slack, email, workshops, executive briefings, texts, and standups.
You leverage automation and AI yourself to execute with clarity, speed, and alignment..
Data guides your decisions.
You interpret usage patterns, account signals, and customer behavior to inform strategy.
You collaborate with data science and RevOps to shape MVP → mature data products and workflows.
You combine early data signals with strong first principles thinking to make decisions and evaluate progress.
You embody our cultural anchors
You lead customer engagements with clarity, dependability, approachable expertise, focusing on delivery meaningful value.
Internally you exercise good judgement and demonstrate our values of collaboration with purpose, accountability, recognition.

What You’ll Do


Guide Customers’ Automation Strategy
Build deep relationships that uncover goals, challenges, and priorities.
Advise on automation governance, best practices, and cross-functional program structure, process and design.
Help customers set goals and adopt AI-orchestrated processes across lines of business.
Discover and Deploy High-Value Use Cases
Identify impactful automation opportunities through exec alignment, influencer mapping, and citizen-developer input.
Work with customer stakeholders, SAs and TAMs to co-design implementation patterns that ensure scalability and long-term success.
Translate business problems into orchestration strategies that deliver measurable value.
Capture the prior state and descriptive metrics that help you quantify the impact of the work.


Drive Customer Outcomes
Demonstrate ROI and unlock new value paths through expanded automation usage.
Accelerate renewal and expansion by deepening customer maturity and outcomes.
Collaborate Across Zapier
Partner with Sales, Support, Product, and others to deliver a unified customer experience.
Share insights that influence roadmap decisions, positioning and GTM strategy
Build a World-Class Program
Help scale the methodologies, frameworks, and assets that define automation strategy at Zapier.
Do the best work of your life as we build Zapier for Enterprise from a subset of the business to the default choice of enterprises globally

How To Apply

At Zapier, we believe that diverse perspectives and experiences make us better, which is why we have a non-standard application process designed to promote inclusion and equity. We’re looking for the best fit for each of our roles, regardless of the type of companies in your background, so we encourage you to apply even if your skills and experiences don’t exactly match the job description. All we ask is that you answer a few in-depth questions in our application that would typically be asked at the start of an interview process. This helps speed things up by letting us get to know you and your skillset a bit better right out of the gate. Please be sure to answer each question; the resume and CV fields are optional.

Education is not a requirement for our roles; however, if you receive an offer, you will need to include your most recent educational experience as part of our background check process.

After you apply, you are going to hear back from us—even if we don’t see an immediate fit with our team. In fact, throughout the process, we strive to never go more than seven days without letting you know the status of your application. We know we’ll make mistakes from time to time, so if you ever have questions about where you stand or about the process, just ask your recruiter!

Zapier is an equal-opportunity employer and we’re excited to work with talented and empathetic people of all identities. Zapier does not discriminate based on someone’s identity in any aspect of hiring or employment as required by law and in line with our commitment to Diversity, Inclusion, Belonging and Equity. Our code of conduct provides a beacon for the kind of company we strive to be, and we celebrate our differences because those differences are what allow us to make a product that serves a global user base. Zapier will consider all qualified applicants, including those with criminal histories, consistent with applicable laws.

Zapier prioritizes the security of our customers’ information and is dedicated to adhering to all applicable data privacy laws. You can review our privacy policy here.

Zapier is committed to inclusion. As part of this commitment, Zapier welcomes applications from individuals with disabilities and will work to provide reasonable accommodations. If reasonable accommodations are needed to participate in the job application or interview process, please contact jobs@zapier.com.

Application Deadline

The anticipated application window is 30 days from the date job is posted, unless the number of applicants requires it to close sooner or later, or if the position is filled.

Even though we’re an all-remote company, we still need to be thoughtful about where we have Zapiens working. Check out this resource for a list of countries where we currently cannot have Zapiens permanently working.

description:
About the job

Responsibilities

About the Team

The commerce industry has seen tremendous growth in recent years and has become a hotly contested space amongst leading Internet companies, and its future growth cannot be underestimated. We aim to bring discovery, inspiration, and joy back to shopping by making TikTok the commerce channel of choice for merchants, creators, and affiliates.

You've seen the virality and impact of #TikTokMadeMeBuyIt. Come join the team that is helping scale that phenomenon, and pioneer Content & Creator commerce in the LATAM. We aim to bring discovery, inspiration, and joy back to shopping by making TikTok the leading, on-trend commerce platform of choice for brands, creators, and shoppers. We are an entrepreneurial team, passionate about shaping the future of shopping.

The TikTok Shop LATAM operation team aims to deliver high quality products together with a unique customer experience via TikTok. The team is responsible for building the TikTok Ecommerce partnership for the TikTok e-commerce business in the US. With more than 1 billion loyal users globally, we believe TikTok is an ideal platform to deliver a brand new and better e-commerce experience to our users. We are looking for passionate and talented people to join us to build an e-commerce ecosystem that is innovative, secure and intuitive for our users together.

As a project intern, you will have the opportunity to engage in impactful short-term projects that provide you with a glimpse of professional real-world experience. You will gain practical skills through on-the-job learning in a fast-paced work environment and develop a deeper understanding of your career interests.

Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis - we encourage you to apply early.

Successful candidates must be able to commit to at least 3 months long internship period.

Responsibilities:


 Take ownership of business development by leading lead mining, conducting in-depth lead validation, and executing strategic seller outreach to drive pipeline growth.
 Conduct analysis of the seller onboarding funnel, identify bottlenecks through data-driven insights, and formulate actionable optimization strategies to improve conversion rates.
 Deeply engage in the design and delivery of seller training programs and support initiatives, including developing campaign frameworks, curating webinar content, and providing personalized guidance to enhance seller performance.
 Plan and execute operational strategies, content roadmaps, and growth initiatives for assigned seller segments, aligning with business objectives to drive scale and engagement.
 Act as a key liaison in meetings with partners, contribute to strategic discussions, and lead cross-team communication to ensure alignment on goals and seamless execution of collaborative projects.
 Analyze and interpret store performance data to identify gaps and opportunities, provide actionable recommendations, and support measurable results.
Support the development of strategies to grow store sales and identify key actions to improve results.


Qualifications

Minimum Qualifications:


 Applicants are kindly requested to submit an English-language resume
 Currently pursuing an Undergraduate/Master in Business or a related major.
 Quick learner, proactive and resilient. Ability to thrive in ambiguity and adjust fast to change.
 Structured thinking, good data sense, very strong analytical skills
 Excellent communication and cross-team collaboration, skilled at moving things forward.
 Passion for business and aim for higher goals.
 Strong learning attitude, positive mindset, and willingness to contribute.


Preferred Qualification:


 Familiarity with TikTok content ecosystem, shoppable content, and brand-creator partnerships is a plus for TikTok.


By submitting an application for this role, you accept and agree to our global applicant privacy policy, which may be accessed here: https://careers.tiktok.com/legal/privacy

Job Information

About TikTok

TikTok is the leading destination for short-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo.

Why Join Us

Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy - a mission we work towards every day.

We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us.

Diversity & Inclusion

TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voices and to creating an environment that reflects the many communities we reach. We are passionate about this and hope you are too.

TikTok Accommodation

TikTok is committed to providing reasonable accommodations in our recruitment processes for candidates with disabilities, pregnancy, sincerely held religious beliefs or other reasons protected by applicable laws. If you need assistance or a reasonable accommodation, please reach out to us at https://tinyurl.com/RA-request

description:
About the job

Description

We are seeking a leader with procurement experience to strategically source Auxiliary equipment and services for fulfillment operations within CAPEX Equipment category. The person in this role will coordinate with several teams across Amazon and will interact with suppliers to satisfy organizational requirements. The position will work and partner with multiple teams to develop and execute robust category sourcing strategies, deliver savings, improve supply chain resilience and productivity across the category. The ideal candidate is a self-starter, and a creative, critical thinker with an strong motivation to learn. You must be an effective communicator and be able to work independently and as a leader of peers to generate high-quality work product in a collaborative, creative, ever-changing, and sometimes ambiguous environment.

Key job responsibilities


 Establish multi year category sourcing strategies, lead cross-functional strategic sourcing teams, negotiate with suppliers and execute contracts to optimize Amazon’s competitive position
 Drive appropriate relationships with category suppliers, with a principal objective of achieving the optimal solution and cost of ownership that meets the business’ goals supported by a culture of continuous improvement with performance benchmarks in use
 Manage category suppliers to proactively identify, monitor and optimize opportunities
 Act as a market intelligence provider regarding categories; anticipate strategic opportunities and keep key stakeholders informed
 Drive category suppliers in Supplier Relationship Management activities to mitigate risk, improve performance and add value to Amazon
 Identify, achieve and report on savings and value targets for category sourcing activities
 Align with internal customers and understand the business application of the category


Key Performance Indicators


 Earn the trust of key stakeholders to drive alignment with the business
 Create a robust category strategy in conjunction with internal customers to ensure there is a comprehensive, unified approach for the category.
 Achieve yearly cost saving or value targets appropriate to the category aligned with annual budget processes.
 Define and report on key metrics that measure effectiveness of supply and cost savings
 Champion AI workflows and procurement standards


A day in the life

As a CAPEX Equipment procurement leader at Amazon, you'll be at the forefront of driving value and innovation across our Customer Fulfillment network. You'll be constantly balancing short-term operational needs with long-term strategic goals, always with an eye on how your decisions impact Amazon's ability to delight customers.In this role, you're not just managing spend – you're shaping the future of e-commerce fulfillment. Your work will have a tangible impact on Amazon's bottom line and will play a crucial role in our mission to be Earth's most customer-centric company.

About The Team

We're seeking leaders to join our Global Procurement Operations (GPO) CAPEX Equipment Category Team where strategic thinking meets operational excellence to power Amazon's fulfillment network. As part of our diverse procurement team, you'll help manage critical categories that keep our vast logistics operations running efficiently.

What Makes Our Team Special


 Collaborative culture where innovative ideas thrive
 Data-driven decision making approach
 Opportunity to make significant impact on global operations
 Supporting one of the world's largest logistics networks


As we continue growing, we need leaders who can help raise the bar in category sourcing, procurement excellence and drive our fulfillment network forward with passion and purpose.

Basic Qualifications


 3+ years of working cross functionally with tech and non-tech teams experience
 3+ years of defining and implementing process improvement initiatives using data and metrics experience
 2+ years of relevant strategic sourcing and category management in indirect categories such as professional services, support or maintenance spend, supply chain, forecasting, manufacturing, sourcing/procurement, or vendor management experience
 Experience defining program requirements and using data and metrics to determine improvements
 Bachelor's degree
 Speak, write, and read fluently in English
 1+ years of experience to detail/write/simplify prompts and have ability to leverage Amazon internal AI tools, flows and bots


Preferred Qualifications


 3+ years of driving end to end delivery, and communicating results to senior leadership experience
 3+ years of driving process improvements experience
 Experience in stakeholder management, dealing with multiple stakeholders at varied levels of the organization
 Experience building processes, project management, and schedules


Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.


Company - Servicios Comerciales Amazon Mexico S. de R.L. de C.V.

description:
About the job

We're looking for a highly organized, proactive Marketing Coordinator who enjoys turning ideas into action. This is a hands-on role for someone who thrives on execution, collaboration, and keeping multiple initiatives moving simultaneously.

You'll work closely with the Chief Revenue Officer, Head of Product, and members of the Growth team to coordinate marketing activities that support business development, product marketing, thought leadership, and brand awareness.

Position Summary

The Marketing Coordinator is responsible for executing and coordinating day-to-day marketing initiatives that support company growth. This role serves as the operational hub for marketing, ensuring campaigns, content, events, and cross-functional projects stay organized, on schedule, and aligned with business priorities.

Success in this role comes from being resourceful, organized, and action oriented. You don't need to own the marketing strategy, but you do need to know how to bring it to life through thoughtful execution and strong project coordination.

Key Responsibilities

Campaign Execution


Coordinate and execute marketing campaigns across digital, partner, and event channels. 
Maintain campaign calendars, timelines, and project plans. 
Coordinate campaign assets and ensure deliverables are completed on schedule. 
Support account based marketing (ABM) initiatives and targeted outreach campaigns. 
Assist with partner marketing initiatives and joint marketing programs. 


Content & Communications


Draft and coordinate blogs, newsletters, email campaigns, social media content, and website updates. 
Coordinate executive thought leadership initiatives, including blogs, webinars, speaking opportunities, industry recognition programs, and social content. 
Assist in developing customer success stories, case studies, sales collateral, presentations, and marketing materials. 
Help ensure all content aligns with Nimble Gravity's messaging, positioning, and brand standards. 


Product & Growth Marketing


Partner with the Head of Product to develop and execute go to market communications for new offerings, capabilities, and product enhancements. 
Support the Growth team with campaign materials, messaging, presentations, and sales enablement resources. 
Conduct light market and competitive research to support marketing initiatives. 


Events & Partner Programs


Coordinate webinars, conferences, customer events, and partner marketing activities. 
Manage event logistics, registrations, promotional materials, and post event communications. 
Support follow up activities and reporting for marketing events. 


Marketing Operations


Coordinate projects across internal stakeholders and external vendors. 
Track deliverables, manage timelines, and proactively identify roadblocks. 
Maintain marketing asset libraries, templates, and project documentation. 
Help improve marketing processes, workflows, and team organization. 


Reporting


Track campaign performance and marketing metrics. 
Prepare weekly marketing updates and monthly performance summaries. 
Identify opportunities to improve marketing effectiveness and execution. 


Qualifications


2 to 4 years of experience in marketing, communications, or a related field. 
Strong organizational and project management skills. 
Excellent written and verbal communication skills. 
Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast paced environment. 
Strong attention to detail and follow through. 
Comfortable collaborating across multiple teams and stakeholders. 
Experience with Microsoft 365, LinkedIn, Canva, HubSpot, WordPress, or similar marketing tools is preferred. 


Success in This Role

You'll be successful if you:


Keep marketing initiatives organized and moving without constant direction. 
Deliver high quality work on time and with attention to detail. 
Build strong working relationships with leadership, Growth team, and other stakeholders. 
Take ownership of projects and follow through from planning to completion. 
Continuously look for ways to improve processes, organization, and team efficiency. 
Help create a consistent, professional, and impactful marketing presence across every customer touchpoint. 


Who You Are


You're a doer. You enjoy bringing structure to moving pieces, keeping projects on track, and helping teams execute great ideas. You're naturally curious, collaborative, and dependable, and you're willing to jump in wherever needed. 
You don't wait to be told every next step. You anticipate needs, communicate proactively, and take pride in delivering work that helps the business grow. 
If you're looking for a role where you can make an impact across every aspect of marketing while working alongside product and revenue leaders, we'd love to meet you. 


About Nimble Gravity

Nimble Gravity is an AI and data consulting firm that helps organizations solve complex business problems through modern data, analytics, and AI solutions. We partner with enterprise clients to transform data into business value, and we're growing our marketing team to support that mission.

Nimble Gravity is an Equal Opportunity Employer and considers applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, orientation, national origin, age, disability, genetics, or any other basis forbidden under federal, state, or local law. Nimble Gravity considers all qualified applicants.

description:
About the job

Descripción Empresa

El Puerto de Liverpool es la empresa líder en tiendas departamentales en México. CON MÁS DE 170 AÑOS EN EL MERCADO, nos dedicamos a facilitar la selección de productos y brindarles el mejor servicio a las familias mexicanas, esperando superar las expectativas de calidad, moda, valor y atención dentro de un ambiente agradable.

Contamos con cinco unidades de negocio: Tiendas Liverpool, Tiendas Suburbia, Centros Comerciales Galerías, Boutiques y Negocios Financieros, componiendo una empresa sólida, que ofrece a sus colaboradores estabilidad financiera y el orgullo de pertenecer a una EMPRESA 100% MEXICANA.

NUESTRA MISIÓN ES SERVIR AL CLIENTE EN TODO LUGAR, TODOS LOS DÍAS, TODA LA VIDA, nuestra visión es ser la mejor opción en servicio, surtido y valor. Nuestros valores nos guían hacia el trabajo en equipo, productividad, innovación e integridad.

Misión Del Cargo

Liderar la estrategia de marketing y gestión de proyectos por categoría para maximizar el posicionamiento y la participación de mercado.

Funciones Del Cargo

Gestión de Proyectos: Estructurar, priorizar y dar seguimiento diario a las iniciativas de marketing asegurando entregables puntuales.

Socio Estratégico Comercial: Traducir las necesidades de negocio del área de compras en planos de marketing viables y medibles.

Análisis de Mercado: Utilizar insights de fuentes externas para robustecer la planeación y la toma de decisiones de la categoría.

Monitoreo de Indicadores de Marca: Evaluar el éxito de las iniciativas mediante KPIs de negocio ( Share of Market , Top of Mind y consideración de marca).

Socio de Compras: Te sentarás con los equipos comerciales y de compras para entender sus objetivos de negocio y traducirlos en un plan de marketing robusto.

Control del Cronograma: Revisarás el estatus de los entregables de agencias y equipos internos. Si algo se está retrasando, levantarás la mano, reestructurarás prioridades y mitigarás el riesgo.

Análisis del Entorno: Consultarás bases de datos de mercado para entender qué está pasando allá afuera y cómo podemos ganar mayor participación.

Defensa de Estrategia: Presentarás reportes ejecutivos a líderes comerciales, argumentando con datos por qué una iniciativa funciona y cómo impacta en la mente del consumidor.

Lugar de trabajo: Santa Fe

Decide el horario que mejor se adapte a tus necesidades:

Lunes a Jueves corporativo 8:00 a 14:00 hrs. / remoto 16:00 a 18:00 hrs. / Viernes: corporativo 8:00 a 14:00 hrs.

Inglés B2 (Conversacional)

title: Salesforce Marketing Cloud Consultant
skills: Analyze campaign briefs and business requirements to design scalable solutions in Salesforce Marketing Cloud.
Build, configure, and manage multi-channel customer journeys using Journey Builder.
Develop audience segmentation and targeting logic using SQL and Data Extensions.
Create and manage personalized and dynamic campaign content using AMPscript and Content Builder.
Support campaign execution through Automation Studio workflows, data preparation, and deployment activities.
Build and maintain Data Extensions, ensuring appropriate structure, data quality, and campaign readiness.
Develop Cloud Pages, forms, and landing page experiences when required by campaign needs.
Translate business requirements into efficient technical solutions aligned with campaign goals and platform capabilities.
Collaborate with client stakeholders, internal teams, and cross-functional partners to ensure successful project delivery.
Participate in campaign scoping, solution assessment, and effort estimation.
Troubleshoot issues related to campaign setup, personalization, audience selection, and execution workflows.
Contribute to quality, consistency, and operational excellence across campaign development and deployment processes.
description:
About the job

Introduction

A career in IBM Consulting is built on long-term client relationships and close collaboration worldwide. You’ll work with leading companies across industries, helping them shape their hybrid cloud and AI journeys. With support from our strategic partners, robust IBM technology, and Red Hat, you’ll have the tools to drive meaningful change and accelerate client impact. At IBM Consulting, curiosity fuels success. You’ll be encouraged to challenge the norm, explore new ideas, and create innovative solutions that deliver real results. Our culture of growth and empathy focuses on your long-term career development while valuing your unique skills and experiences.

At IBM, we help organizations create meaningful, data-driven customer experiences through scalable digital marketing solutions. We are looking for a Salesforce Marketing Cloud Consultant who brings strong platform expertise, a consulting mindset, and the ability to turn business needs into effective campaign solutions.

This is an exciting opportunity for a marketing automation professional who enjoys working at the intersection of technology, customer engagement, and problem-solving. In this role, you will partner with clients, internal stakeholders, and cross-functional teams to design and deliver personalized, multi-channel campaigns that drive measurable business impact.

Your Role And Responsibilities

As a Salesforce Marketing Cloud Consultant, you will be responsible for designing, building, and supporting marketing automation solutions within Salesforce Marketing Cloud. You will work closely with stakeholders to understand campaign objectives, evaluate technical requirements, and deliver scalable, high-quality executions across channels.

Key Responsibilities Include


Analyze campaign briefs and business requirements to design scalable solutions in Salesforce Marketing Cloud.
Build, configure, and manage multi-channel customer journeys using Journey Builder.
Develop audience segmentation and targeting logic using SQL and Data Extensions.
Create and manage personalized and dynamic campaign content using AMPscript and Content Builder.
Support campaign execution through Automation Studio workflows, data preparation, and deployment activities.
Build and maintain Data Extensions, ensuring appropriate structure, data quality, and campaign readiness.
Develop Cloud Pages, forms, and landing page experiences when required by campaign needs.
Translate business requirements into efficient technical solutions aligned with campaign goals and platform capabilities.
Collaborate with client stakeholders, internal teams, and cross-functional partners to ensure successful project delivery.
Participate in campaign scoping, solution assessment, and effort estimation.
Troubleshoot issues related to campaign setup, personalization, audience selection, and execution workflows.
Contribute to quality, consistency, and operational excellence across campaign development and deployment processes.


Preferred Education

None

Required Technical And Professional Expertise

Advanced experience in Salesforce Marketing Cloud, leading the design, build, and optimization of marketing automation campaigns, including campaign execution and management.

Strong expertise in SQL and data modeling, including audience segmentation, Data Extensions management, and data relationship design.

Proven ability to design and execute multi-channel customer journeys using Journey Builder, along with automation of workflows through Automation Studio.

Solid experience in dynamic content personalization using AMPscript, combined with hands-on work in Content Builder and knowledge of HTML/CSS for email development and troubleshooting.

Ability to translate business requirements into scalable technical solutions, with a strong understanding of integrations, data flows, and collaboration with cross-functional stakeholders.

Preferred Technical And Professional Experience

Salesforce Marketing Cloud certification

Experience with Cloud Pages, microsites, and user input forms

Knowledge of Server-Side JavaScript

Experience with APIs, integrations, and tools such as Postman

Familiarity with Email Studio and best practices for email campaign execution

Understanding of email deliverability and digital marketing compliance best practices

Experience supporting complex marketing automation solutions in a consulting or client-facing environment

Experience in solution assessment and effort estimation

Ability to provide guidance and support to junior team members

Exposure to Agile ways of working

title: Senior Google Media Buyer
description:
About the job

Senior Google Media Buyer — Google Ads

We're a performance marketing agency that genuinely enjoys our work and the clients we work with. Our portfolio includes some of the highest-growth businesses in the US. We manage over $5 million in direct monthly spend and support more than $10 million in total media allocation across our client base. This role is unique, and the right person brings maniacal execution, a real love of media buying, and the curiosity to understand the businesses behind the accounts.




The Role

You'll own a portfolio of Google Ads accounts requiring deep strategy and hands-on execution across Search, Shopping, YouTube, and Performance Max. You'll need fluency in the tangential tools that make these campaigns actually work — Google Merchant Center, Symprosis, GA4, GTM. Your job is to run accounts to hit business outcomes, not just platform metrics. You'll be expected to own accounts independently within 30 days, with collaborative feedback along the way.

This role is not for someone who treats media buying as a necessary evil or who isn't comfortable being held accountable for results across every account they touch. It's for someone who has strong opinions about match type strategy, who can diagnose a Performance Max asset group issue on sight, and who gets genuinely frustrated when a Shopping feed isn't structured correctly.




What You'll Do

Build and manage campaigns across Search, Shopping, YouTube, and Performance Max with budgets exceeding $200K per month per account. Develop keyword strategies, bid structures, and audience signals that drive profitable scale, not just volume. Execute and iterate Smart Bidding strategies and know when the data says to override them. Write RSA headlines and descriptions grounded in customer psychology, not just keyword insertion. Build Shopping feeds structured to win — title optimization, custom labels, product groupings — and troubleshoot them when they break. Construct Performance Max campaigns with deliberate asset groups and audience signals, not default settings. Monitor performance daily, identify what's degrading before it becomes a problem, act on it without being asked, and build reports that clearly communicate the impact you're driving.




What You Need

4+ years of hands-on Google Ads management at $300K+ per month — not oversight, not reporting, actual buying. Deep Shopping experience including Merchant Center configuration, feed optimization, supplemental feeds, and disapproval resolution. Strong working knowledge of Performance Max including asset group strategy, feed integration for eCommerce, and audience signal layering. Proficiency with GA4, conversion tracking setup, and GTM. Advanced Excel for performance analysis including pivot tables, campaign-level breakdowns, and pattern identification across large data sets. The ability to write compelling ad copy without a copywriter holding your hand.




Who This is Not For

If your Google Ads experience is primarily Search and you haven't managed Shopping or Performance Max at scale, this isn't the right fit. If you need daily direction on what to prioritize, this isn't the right fit. If your idea of optimization is adjusting bids once a week and pulling a report, this isn't the right fit. We move fast, we expect ownership, and we don't have the bandwidth to onboard someone in the fundamentals.

title: Account Manager, SMB (Ad-Sales) - SpLATAM
description:
About the job

Responsibilities
About the team:
TikTok's SMB team aims to help businesses, agencies and brands of all sizes unleash their creative side, connect with our audience, or perhaps be discovered! From designing scaled sales and service delivery programs to ensuring our customers have tailored tools and products at their fingertips, our SMB team combines operational expertise with a customer-centric mindset to help businesses achieve their marketing goals on TikTok, no matter how big or small.

About the Role:
The SMB Account Manager will be a crucial member of a growing team that will support sales in meeting its objectives and providing best in class customer service to clients. This role is responsible for partnering with the SMB clients and internal stakeholders to drive revenue by negotiating and optimizing complex opportunities and use data and analytics to build a consultative solution for our customers. The SMB Account Manager will establish and strengthen key client relationships with a focus on driving revenue, advertiser education & advertiser satisfaction. Success in this position requires a focus on client service, the ability to thrive in a dynamic work environment, and the ability to help shape and leverage scalable solutions to grow and support SMB customers.

Responsibilities:
- Drive the TikTok SMB business and revenue by developing partnerships with SMB clients and agencies.
- Ensure that our clients receive the highest level of sales and operational customer service.
- Update clients on TikTok products and developments and educate clients and agencies on best practices.
- Use a consultative approach by offering strategic media solutions with all clients.
- Develop and implement best practices for client interaction, sales and services.
- Create persuasive sales presentations using market trends and case studies.
- Create compelling sales propositions utilizing internal and external data for respective regions.
- Liaise with cross-functional teams on structuring and executing operational and strategic initiatives.

Qualifications
Minimum Qualification(s)
- 5 years of experience in marketing, brand advertising, media sales, and/or online advertising.
- Experience in social media, mobile apps, digital and mobile marketing.
- Knowledge of a self-serve platform, auction, or programmatic ad products.
- Proven performance, winning the support of key stakeholders, and track record of reaching and exceeding sales goals.
- Ability to perform well in a highly dynamic, rapidly changing environment. Also, a creative, outside-the-box thinker and strategist, a self-starter, and a team player.
- Passion about TikTok marketing, and up for the challenge of building something from the bottom up.
- Excellent verbal and written Spanish & English language skills.

Preferred Qualification(s)
- Proactive attitude with excellent data analytical skills in Excel.
- SQL skills are a plus

About TikTok
TikTok is the leading destination for short-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo.


Why Join Us
Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy - a mission we work towards every day.
We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us.

Diversity & Inclusion
TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voices and to creating an environment that reflects the many communities we reach. We are passionate about this and hope you are too.


TikTok Accommodation
TikTok is committed to providing reasonable accommodations in our recruitment processes for candidates with disabilities, pregnancy, sincerely held religious beliefs or other reasons protected by applicable laws. If you need assistance or a reasonable accommodation, please reach out to us at https://tinyurl.com/RA-request

title: Creative Performance Strategist
skills: Creative Performance Analysis & Reporting
Analyze the performance of creative assets across various platforms (Meta, TikTok, YouTube, etc.) to identify winning patterns and elements.
Evaluate creative components such as hooks, visuals, copy, and CTAs to determine their impact on key performance indicators (KPIs) like CTR, CPA, and ROAS.
Build and maintain comprehensive dashboards and reports that focus specifically on creative efficacy.
Strategic Bridge & Briefing
Act as the primary liaison between the Media/Data teams and the Creative/Production teams.
Translate media performance data into creative "lessons learned" and actionable creative briefs for future production.
Collaborate with the Creative/Pencil Lead to integrate AI-driven insights into the creative workflow.
Testing & Optimization Frameworks
Develop and manage a robust creative testing roadmap, including A/B testing and multivariate testing protocols.
Provide data-backed recommendations for creative iterations and optimizations.
Oversee the metadata tagging process for creative assets to ensure granular data collection and analysis.
Cross-Functional Collaboration
Participate in regular cross-departmental syncs to align creative production with media buying strategies.
Educate the creative team on performance metrics and how their work impacts the bottom line.
Stay ahead of industry trends and platform updates to ensure our creative strategies remain cutting-edge.
description:
About the job

At Jellyfish we believe that our individual differences are our greatest assets. We are passionate about empowering every employee to reach their full potential and contribute their unique perspectives to our collaborative environment. We are an equal opportunity employer and firmly believe that a diverse and inclusive workforce drives innovation and leads to better solutions. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, or any other personal characteristics that make them who they are.

Jellyfish is a global digital marketing agency; a unique fusion of tech enthusiasts, creative minds, and media and data experts all united to empower our clients along their digital journey. Our commitment to embracing diverse perspectives fuels our innovation and strategies that challenge the status quo, reinvent media activation, and craft influential stories for our global clients and their customers. Join us in shaping a future where business growth and personal fulfilment go hand in hand.

Job Description

We are seeking a highly analytical and creative-minded Creative Performance Strategist to join our team. This role is designed to bridge the gap between media performance and creative production. You will be responsible for dissecting creative performance data to understand why certain ads perform better than others, translating those insights into actionable strategies for our creative and media teams. You will work closely with our Creative/Pencil Lead and Media Buyers to optimize our creative output based on rigorous data analysis.

Key Responsibilities


 Creative Performance Analysis & Reporting
Analyze the performance of creative assets across various platforms (Meta, TikTok, YouTube, etc.) to identify winning patterns and elements.
Evaluate creative components such as hooks, visuals, copy, and CTAs to determine their impact on key performance indicators (KPIs) like CTR, CPA, and ROAS.
Build and maintain comprehensive dashboards and reports that focus specifically on creative efficacy.
 Strategic Bridge & Briefing
Act as the primary liaison between the Media/Data teams and the Creative/Production teams.
Translate media performance data into creative "lessons learned" and actionable creative briefs for future production.
Collaborate with the Creative/Pencil Lead to integrate AI-driven insights into the creative workflow.
 Testing & Optimization Frameworks
Develop and manage a robust creative testing roadmap, including A/B testing and multivariate testing protocols.
Provide data-backed recommendations for creative iterations and optimizations.
Oversee the metadata tagging process for creative assets to ensure granular data collection and analysis.
 Cross-Functional Collaboration
Participate in regular cross-departmental syncs to align creative production with media buying strategies.
Educate the creative team on performance metrics and how their work impacts the bottom line.
Stay ahead of industry trends and platform updates to ensure our creative strategies remain cutting-edge.


Requirements & Skills

Experience: 3-5 years of experience in media analysis, digital marketing, or a performance-driven creative role. Advanced English proficiency. 

Analytical Skills: Proficiency in data analysis tools (Excel, SQL, Google Data Studio, Tableau) and platform native analytics.

Platform Knowledge: Deep understanding of Paid Social and Programmatic ads platforms

Creative Intuition: A strong grasp of storytelling, visual design principles, and what resonates with audiences on social media.

Tool Proficiency: Experience with AI-driven creative tools (e.g., Pencil) and creative management platforms is highly desirable.

Note: We emphasise skills, expertise and behavioural attributes over years of experience and traditional degrees. If you want to join our collaborative team, we invite you to apply today with your resume in English.

Additional Information

Join Jellyfish and experience a workplace where we prioritise your growth, celebrate your contributions, and empower you to tailor your work environment to suit your needs.

💰 Savings fund: You'll save 5% of your monthly income and Jellyfish will contribute the same amount you save to your savings fund.

💫 Custom Work Environment: Work remotely for up to 60% of your days and shape your day between 8am. and 6:30pm with flexible working hours.

🏥 Health Insurance: We provide a private health insurance policy that includes visual and dental plan coverage.

Unfortunately, there has been an increase in fake recruiters impersonating Jellyfish and unlawfully using our brand name. If you are unsure if an email with a job offer you have received is genuinely from Jellyfish, or if you suspect any fraudulent activity, please report it to talentacquisition@jellyfish.com.

Together, with the professionals and institutions we serve, we help uphold the rule of law, turn the wheels of commerce, catch bad actors, report the facts, and provide trusted, unbiased information to people all over the world.
description:
About the job

Reuters Events is seeking a commercially minded and data-driven Marketing Manager to lead the planning and execution of marketing campaigns across a portfolio of global conferences and content products. Reporting to the Senior Marketing Lead, this role will be responsible for driving audience growth, lead generation, delegate acquisition, and engagement among senior executive communities.

The Marketing Manager will work cross-functionally with product, sales, partnerships, and operations teams to deliver high-impact, multi-channel campaigns that support business growth and strengthen Reuters Events' market presence.

About The Role

As a Marketing Manager within Reuters Events, you will:


Develop and execute integrated marketing campaigns for Reuters Events conferences, digital content, and related products.
Drive delegate acquisition, audience growth, and lead generation through targeted go-to-market strategies.
Identify and implement innovative ways to reach senior executive audiences across key industry sectors.
Build and manage channel, media, and marketing partnerships to support event promotion and customer acquisition.
Plan and deliver multi-channel campaigns across email, web, content, paid media, partnerships, and social channels.
Monitor, analyse, and optimize campaign performance using tools such as Google Analytics and Salesforce.
Track key marketing KPIs and provide regular reporting and insight to stakeholders.
Collaborate closely with internal teams including sales, content, and event operations to ensure campaign alignment and commercial success.
Support database growth, audience segmentation, and targeted outreach initiatives.
Ensure strong brand positioning and consistent messaging across all marketing activity.


About You

To be a Marketing Manager within Reuters Events, you will likely have:


Proven ability to develop and execute B2B marketing campaigns and strategies, ideally within events, media, or professional services.
Strong background in campaign planning, demand generation, and audience development.
Experience delivering integrated marketing campaigns across multiple channels.
Ability to use data and reporting to evaluate campaign performance and drive optimization.
Familiarity with platforms such as Google Analytics, Salesforce and email marketing systems.
Excellent project management and organizational skills, with the ability to manage multiple campaigns simultaneously.
Strong communication and stakeholder management skills.
Commercially aware, proactive, and comfortable working in a fast-paced environment.


Preferred Experience


Experience marketing to senior executive or C-level audiences.
Experience working with external partners, media partners, or agencies.
Understanding of lead generation and customer acquisition strategies in a B2B environment.


What’s in it For You?


Hybrid Work Model: We’ve adopted a flexible hybrid working environment (2-3 days a week in the office depending on the role) for our office-based roles while delivering a seamless experience that is digitally and physically connected.
Flexibility & Work-Life Balance: Flex My Way is a set of supportive workplace policies designed to help manage personal and professional responsibilities, whether caring for family, giving back to the community, or finding time to refresh and reset. This builds upon our flexible work arrangements, including work from anywhere for up to 8 weeks per year, empowering employees to achieve a better work-life balance.
Career Development and Growth: By fostering a culture of continuous learning and skill development, we prepare our talent to tackle tomorrow’s challenges and deliver real-world solutions. Our Grow My Way programming and skills-first approach ensures you have the tools and knowledge to grow, lead, and thrive in an AI-enabled future.
Industry Competitive Benefits: We offer comprehensive benefit plans to include flexible vacation, two company-wide Mental Health Days off, access to the Headspace app, retirement savings, tuition reimbursement, employee incentive programs, and resources for mental, physical, and financial wellbeing.
Culture: Globally recognized, award-winning reputation for inclusion and belonging, flexibility, work-life balance, and more. We live by our values: Obsess over our Customers, Compete to Win, Challenge (Y)our Thinking, Act Fast / Learn Fast, and Stronger Together.
Social Impact: Make an impact in your community with our Social Impact Institute. We offer employees two paid volunteer days off annually and opportunities to get involved with pro-bono consulting projects and Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) initiatives.
Making a Real-World Impact: We are one of the few companies globally that helps its customers pursue justice, truth, and transparency. Together, with the professionals and institutions we serve, we help uphold the rule of law, turn the wheels of commerce, catch bad actors, report the facts, and provide trusted, unbiased information to people all over the world.


About Us

Thomson Reuters informs the way forward by bringing together the trusted content and technology that people and organizations need to make the right decisions. We serve professionals across legal, tax, accounting, compliance, government, and media. Our products combine highly specialized software and insights to empower professionals with the data, intelligence, and solutions needed to make informed decisions, and to help institutions in their pursuit of justice, truth, and transparency. Reuters, part of Thomson Reuters, is a world leading provider of trusted journalism and news.

We are powered by the talents of 26,000 employees across more than 70 countries, where everyone has a chance to contribute and grow professionally in flexible work environments. At a time when objectivity, accuracy, fairness, and transparency are under attack, we consider it our duty to pursue them. Sound exciting? Join us and help shape the industries that move society forward.

As a global business, we rely on the unique backgrounds, perspectives, and experiences of all employees to deliver on our business goals. To ensure we can do that, we seek talented, qualified employees in all our operations around the world regardless of race, color, sex/gender, including pregnancy, gender identity and expression, national origin, religion, sexual orientation, disability, age, marital status, citizen status, veteran status, or any other protected classification under applicable law. Thomson Reuters is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer providing a drug-free workplace.

We also make reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities and for sincerely held religious beliefs in accordance with applicable law. More information on requesting an accommodation here.

Learn more on how to protect yourself from fraudulent job postings here.

More information about Thomson Reuters can be found on thomsonreuters.com

Experiencia en investigación de mercados, publicidad o marketing

Conocimientos en Power BI


Beneficios

Por eso, ofrecemos: Beneficios de ley y Beneficios superiores que superan los requisitos legales. title: Senior Marketing Tech and CRM Administrator
skills: Serve as the primary SFMC Admin across all business units, managing user accounts, role-based permissions, and security settings.
Oversee multi-Business Unit configurations and maintain overall platform health.
Design, build, and optimize complex Journey Builder flows, Automation Studio workflows, and triggered sends.
Own the troubleshooting of data integrations between SFMC, Salesforce CRM (via Marketing Cloud Connect), and upstream data systems using REST/SOAP APIs.
Oversee the SFMC data model, including Data Extensions, SQL query activities, and Contact Builder relationships.
Ensure strict data hygiene, suppression governance, and accurate targeting for all global sends.
Lead Content Builder strategy, providing advanced support for AMPscript-driven personalization and dynamic email design systems.
Act as the principal technical point of contact for our global user community, mentoring junior administrators and providing platform best practices.
Partner with leadership to evaluate emerging capabilities such as Marketing Cloud Next, Personalization Builder, and Einstein AI.
Develop and execute Proof of Concepts (POCs) to inform build-vs-buy decisions and platform roadmaps.
Experience: 5 - 8 years in Marketing Automation/Operations, with at least 3 years specifically administering SFMC at an enterprise scale.

description:
About the job

Company Description

At Nielsen, we are passionate about our work to power a better media future for all people by providing powerful insights that drive client decisions and deliver extraordinary results. Our talented, global workforce is dedicated to capturing audience engagement with content - wherever and whenever it’s consumed. Together, we are proudly rooted in our deep legacy as we stand at the forefront of the media revolution. When you join Nielsen, you will join a dynamic team committed to excellence, perseverance, and the ambition to make an impact together. We champion you, because when you succeed, we do too. We enable your best to power our future.

Job Description

We are seeking a Senior Marketing Automation Administrator with deep Salesforce Marketing Cloud (SFMC) expertise to lead the day-to-day management, configuration, and strategic optimization of our marketing automation ecosystem.

As our SFMC technical authority, you will partner with marketing leadership to architect scalable journeys, harden platform governance, and drive continuous improvement across our MarTech stack. You will ensure platform stability, own complex data integrations, and mentor a global team of administrators to execute high-impact campaigns.

What You’ll Do

System Administration & Governance


Serve as the primary SFMC Admin across all business units, managing user accounts, role-based permissions, and security settings.
Oversee multi-Business Unit configurations and maintain overall platform health.


Architecting Workflows & Integrations


Design, build, and optimize complex Journey Builder flows, Automation Studio workflows, and triggered sends.
Own the troubleshooting of data integrations between SFMC, Salesforce CRM (via Marketing Cloud Connect), and upstream data systems using REST/SOAP APIs.


Data & Audience Management


Oversee the SFMC data model, including Data Extensions, SQL query activities, and Contact Builder relationships.
Ensure strict data hygiene, suppression governance, and accurate targeting for all global sends.


Advanced Support & Enablement


Lead Content Builder strategy, providing advanced support for AMPscript-driven personalization and dynamic email design systems.
Act as the principal technical point of contact for our global user community, mentoring junior administrators and providing platform best practices.


Innovation & Strategy


Partner with leadership to evaluate emerging capabilities such as Marketing Cloud Next, Personalization Builder, and Einstein AI.
Develop and execute Proof of Concepts (POCs) to inform build-vs-buy decisions and platform roadmaps.


Qualifications

Professional Background


Experience: 5 - 8 years in Marketing Automation/Operations, with at least 3 years specifically administering SFMC at an enterprise scale.
Product Owner Mindset: You don't just react to tickets; you proactively drive platform sustainability and operational efficiency.
Communication: Professional-level English proficiency is required for global collaboration and technical documentation.


Additional Information

Please be aware that job-seekers may be at risk of targeting by scammers seeking personal data or money. Nielsen recruiters will only contact you through official job boards, LinkedIn, or email with a nielsen.com domain. Be cautious of any outreach claiming to be from Nielsen via other messaging platforms or personal email addresses. Always verify that email communications come from an @nielsen.com address. If you're unsure about the authenticity of a job offer or communication, please contact Nielsen directly through our official website or verified social media channels.

description:
About the job

Marketing Data Analyst

Why join Aptiv? You'll have the opportunity to work on cutting-edge applications, develop breakthrough technologies, and deliver innovative solutions to some of the world’s leading automotive brands. See your work come to life on the road—helping make mobility safer, greener, and more connected. Ready to shape the future of mobility with us?

YOUR ROLE

We are looking for a Marketing Data Specialist to join our Digital Technology and Analytics team to help us build, maintain and improve the data foundations that power customer engagement and marketing reporting. In this role, you will work closely with data, marketing, sales, product & IT teams to ensure that data from our stack is available, accurate & fit for use. This is a cross-functional role that will translate stakeholder needs into data solutions across our tech stack, data warehouse and BI layer ensuring our teams have the trusted data they need to provide insights back to the business. If you bring strong data experience and are motivated to grow your career across marketing technology, customer data, and analytics we encourage you to apply.

In your daily job you will:


Working within a cross-functional team to bring to life a next-generation marketing data platform & toolkit
Own and support advanced website tracking and campaign tagging through Google Tag Manager, including tags, triggers, variables, data layer logic and consent-based tracking
Translate business, campaign and reporting requirements from Data Analytics, Sales, Marketing, Product and Events teams into clear tracking, data and audience requirements
Partner with the data engineering team to ensure the Marketing EDW datasets align to business requirements, validating data loads, refresh logic and source-to-warehouse accuracy
Utilize SQL to validate, reconcile and transform marketing and sales datasets into functional data models for reporting.
Work within the tools in our stack including marketing automation and CRM systems to understand, troubleshoot & improve source-system logic as it relates to downstream data and reporting outputs
Establish repeatable processes to monitor, troubleshoot and resolve data quality issues across the full marketing technology stack, including accuracy, completeness, cleanliness and compliance.
Support configuration and maintenance of integrations between tools in our stack, ensuring data flows continuously and smoothly between them.
Support Marketing’s Airtable environment where it connects to campaign execution, operational workflows, customer data and reporting processes
Maintain comprehensive documentation of data processes, tracking logic, validation checks, models and system dependencies.


YOUR BACKGROUND

Key skills and competencies for succeeding in this role are:


Completed bachelor's degree
Proficient in English speaking/reading/writing
+4 years prior professional experience in data engineering, analytics, marketing operations, CRM data or related
Experience building dashboards and/ or reports from multiple data sources
Experience with SQL, Excel (VLOOKUPS/ Pivots etc) and BI tools such as Power BI, Tableau or similar
Experience with relational database concepts, data management and ETL concepts
Experience validating data between source systems, reporting tools and/ or data warehouse environments
Familiarity with consent, privacy and data governance concepts as they relate to digital tracking and customer data
Solid communication and organizational skills, attention to detail and experience working with multiple stakeholders at all levels
Initiative-taker with a demonstrated ability to prioritize and multi-task.


Nice to haves:


Experience working within Marketing, Sales, Commercial or Product teams
Experience with Airtable/ Power Automate or similar
Experience with Salesforce, Marketo, Demandbase, Google Tag Manager or similar
Experience with APIs, Python or scripting languages


WHY JOIN US?


You can grow at Aptiv. Aptiv provides an inclusive work environment where all individuals can grow and develop, regardless of gender, ethnicity or beliefs.
You can have an impact. Safety is a core Aptiv value; we want a safer world for us and our children, one with: Zero fatalities, Zero injuries, Zero accidents.
You have support. We ensure you have the resources and support you need to take care of your family and your physical and mental health with a competitive health insurance package.


APPLY TODAY, AND TOGETHER LET’S CHANGE TOMORROW!

title: Senior Creative Strategist
description:
About the job

The Role

You'll own creative strategy across our full client portfolio — eCommerce, lead generation, and software. That means owning the brief, owning the backlog, owning the UGC creator pipeline, and owning what happens when a creative stops working. You're the person who knows what to test next before anyone asks, and why. This is not a brand strategy role. It's not a creative director role. It's a performance creative role; every concept you develop has a CPA target behind it, and you're responsible for helping hit it.




What You'll Own

Brief Writing and Concept Development: Own the end-to-end briefing process for all creative entering production — static, short-form video, long-form direct response, display, and YouTube. Maintain a rolling backlog with 4+ weeks of net-new concepts brief-ready at all times. Hit monthly throughput targets across the full vertical portfolio. Balance net-new concepts with iteration direction — enough new angles to sustain testing velocity, enough iteration to fully exploit what's already winning. Proactively identify untested formats and underserved verticals and bring them into the queue before someone has to ask.




Performance Intelligence: Analyze creative performance data alongside the paid media team to identify winners, flag fatigue, and determine what to test next. Translate findings into new briefs and updated creative direction — not just observations in a Slack message. Maintain a living creative playbook that documents winning formulas by persona, vertical, and format so the team always starts from accumulated learning, not from zero. Run competitive creative research on a regular cadence and flag emerging opportunities before they're mainstream. Drive cross-vertical learning — when something wins in one vertical, brief it for the others.




UGC Creator and Talent Management: Own the UGC creator pipeline from sourcing through performance review. Maintain the Airtable creator dashboard as the single source of truth — profiles, availability, past performance, brief status, and delivery status. Write creator briefs specific enough to reduce revision cycles and increase the odds of a usable first delivery. Build and manage creator relationships across the full portfolio, not just the highest-volume clients.




What You Need

5+ years of direct response creative strategy experience with hands-on performance media context — you don't need to be a media buyer but you need to think like one. Demonstrated ability to write production-ready briefs that creative teams execute without revision rounds. Performance data fluency — you can read results, identify what's driving them, and translate findings into creative direction. Experience managing UGC creators from sourcing through delivery. The ability to work across multiple verticals and formats simultaneously without losing strategic focus. Strong copywriting ability in scripts, hooks, headlines, and ad copy — this meaningfully accelerates the briefing process and is a real differentiator for this role. Experience leveraging AI tools to push creative strategy further and faster.




What the Day to Day Actually Looks Like

You start the week knowing what's in production, what's in the backlog, and what the data said about last week's creative. You're writing briefs, reviewing creator deliveries, analyzing performance with the media team, and updating the playbook. You're not waiting for someone to bring you a problem — you're identifying it in the data and already briefing the solution. You communicate primarily in Slack, move fast, and write clearly.




Who This is Not For

If your creative strategy experience is primarily brand or awareness, this isn't the right fit. If you need a creative director above you to validate concepts before they go to production, this isn't the right fit. If you've never worked with performance data as a direct input to creative decisions, this isn't the right fit. We need someone who has owned outcomes, not just output.he digital space. The team focuses on developing customized strategies that align with each client’s unique identity, goals, and audiences. By combining data-driven insights with creative innovation, Future of Learning helps businesses navigate and succeed in an ever-evolving digital landscape. Team members collaborate with diverse brands and industries, offering opportunities to work on impactful, forward-thinking initiatives. The organization values adaptability, experimentation, and continuous learning in support of long-term client growth.

Fundada en 2004, OLIVER es la primera y única empresa del mundo especializada en diseñar, crear y gestionar agencias y ecosistemas de marketing internos a medida con el modelo inhouse para distintas marcas. Como parte de Brandtech Group, estamos a la vanguardia en el uso de tecnología de inteligencia artificial para revolucionar la forma en que creamos y entregamos nuestro trabajo. From weekly content performance to campaign post-mortems, audience deep-dives to competitive benchmarking, you'll deliver best-in-class social performance insight that delivers robust and actionable learnings for social teams and practices.
