title: Creative Resource Executive
skills: Assist in the development and implementation of workforce planning strategies aligned with both short-term and long-term business goals.
Help the Resource Management team to identify, acquire, assess, and hire freelance candidates to fill open positions within a company.
Process documentation for freelance set-up to ensure a steam-lined freelance experience.
Helping new freelance employees integrate into the organization.
Process documentation for creator and contractor set-up as well as initiating invoices and payments.
Conduct regular assessments of staffing needs based on current and projected workloads, industry trends, and company growth.
Utilize data analytics and forecasting tools to support staffing predictions and improve resource allocation.
Collaborate with department managers, People team, Integrated Producers and other team members to understand staffing requirements and ensure alignment across teams.
Help facilitate communication across departments to ensure a unified approach to workforce planning.
Evaluate team capacities and suggest areas for improvement to ensure optimal resource utilization.
Work with hiring managers to address skill gaps and explore upskilling opportunities.
Adjusting strategies as needed to meet changing business requirements.
Track and report workforce metrics to leadership, highlighting areas for improvement and talent risk management.
Stay informed on industry trends, emerging technologies, and best practices in workforce planning.
Continuously assess and improve workforce planning processes to adapt to evolving needs.
Support the global Resource Team in building a well-resourced, effective, and productive global team.
1+ years of experience in workforce planning, capacity planning, or a related role within a digital marketing, advertising, or technology business.
Advanced English proficiency.
Experience using Wrike or similar software for resource and workforce management.
Proficiency in data analysis and forecasting tools.
Strong ability to collaborate across teams and departments to optimize resources.
Familiarity with digital marketing processes and workflows, particularly within creative teams.
Basic understanding of project management principles and practices.
Able to take direction and follow guidance given from other team members and the leadership team.
description:
About the job

At Jellyfish we believe that our individual differences are our greatest assets. We are passionate about empowering every employee to reach their full potential and contribute their unique perspectives to our collaborative environment. We are an equal opportunity employer and firmly believe that a diverse and inclusive workforce drives innovation and leads to better solutions. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, or any other personal characteristics that make them who they are.

Jellyfish is a global digital marketing agency; a unique fusion of tech enthusiasts, creative minds, and media and data experts all united to empower our clients along their digital journey. Our commitment to embracing diverse perspectives fuels our innovation and strategies that challenge the status quo, reinvent media activation, and craft influential stories for our global clients and their customers. Join us in shaping a future where business growth and personal fulfilment go hand in hand.

Job Description

Reporting into the Senior Resource Director, as a Resource Executive, you will play a crucial role in ensuring the effective utilization of our Creative team to meet business objectives. You will assist in developing workforce planning strategies, forecasting staffing needs, and optimizing resource allocation to improve operational efficiency. Collaborating closely with our capabilities, you will align workforce strategies with company goals, contribute to a positive work environment, and support the overall success of the organization.

Your Primary Responsibilities Include


Assist in the development and implementation of workforce planning strategies aligned with both short-term and long-term business goals.
Help the Resource Management team to identify, acquire, assess, and hire freelance candidates to fill open positions within a company.
Process documentation for freelance set-up to ensure a steam-lined freelance experience.
Helping new freelance employees integrate into the organization.
Process documentation for creator and contractor set-up as well as initiating invoices and payments.
Conduct regular assessments of staffing needs based on current and projected workloads, industry trends, and company growth.
Utilize data analytics and forecasting tools to support staffing predictions and improve resource allocation.
Collaborate with department managers, People team, Integrated Producers and other team members to understand staffing requirements and ensure alignment across teams.
Help facilitate communication across departments to ensure a unified approach to workforce planning.
Evaluate team capacities and suggest areas for improvement to ensure optimal resource utilization.
Work with hiring managers to address skill gaps and explore upskilling opportunities.
Adjusting strategies as needed to meet changing business requirements.
Track and report workforce metrics to leadership, highlighting areas for improvement and talent risk management.
Stay informed on industry trends, emerging technologies, and best practices in workforce planning.
Continuously assess and improve workforce planning processes to adapt to evolving needs.
Support the global Resource Team in building a well-resourced, effective, and productive global team.


Qualifications


1+ years of experience in workforce planning, capacity planning, or a related role within a digital marketing, advertising, or technology business.
Advanced English proficiency.
Experience using Wrike or similar software for resource and workforce management.
Proficiency in data analysis and forecasting tools.
Strong ability to collaborate across teams and departments to optimize resources.
Familiarity with digital marketing processes and workflows, particularly within creative teams.
Basic understanding of project management principles and practices.
Able to take direction and follow guidance given from other team members and the leadership team.


Note: We emphasise skills, expertise and behavioural attributes over years of experience and traditional degrees. If you want to join our collaborative team, we invite you to apply today with your resume in English.

Additional Information

Join Jellyfish and experience a workplace where we prioritise your growth, celebrate your contributions, and empower you to tailor your work environment to suit your needs.

💰 Savings fund: You'll save 5% of your monthly income and Jellyfish will contribute the same amount you save to your savings fund.

💫 Custom Work Environment: Work remotely for up to 60% of your days and shape your day between 8am. and 6:30pm with flexible working hours.

🏥 Health Insurance: We provide a private health insurance policy that includes visual and dental plan coverage.

Unfortunately, there has been an increase in fake recruiters impersonating Jellyfish and unlawfully using our brand name. If you are unsure if an email with a job offer you have received is genuinely from Jellyfish, or if you suspect any fraudulent activity, please report it to talentacquisition@jellyfish.com.

description:
About the job

Omnicom Media (OM), the media services division of Omnicom Group Inc. (NYSE: OMC) – delivers transformational experiences for consumers, clients, and talent. Powered by the Omni marketing orchestration system, OM connects best-in-class capabilities that enable our full-service media agencies OMD, PHD, and Hearts & Science to deliver more relevant and actionable consumer experiences, more productive and proactive client experiences, and more collaborative and rewarding talent experiences for the more than 24,000 people serving the world’s leading brands in OM agencies around the globe.

Manager, Growth Analytics

Position Summary

We are looking for a Manager, Growth Analytics to join our team! We are looking for ambitious, analytical, hands-on problem solver who can manage multiple projects. This individual should have a strong interest in media, marketing and business intelligence.

The role requires the ability to actively apply the agency’s proprietary ad effectiveness and measurement tools, as well as 3rd party vendor solutions, to determine the impact of advertising on the client’s business. The position also requires the ability to understand the strategic communications needs of client advertisers and align these needs to the day-to-day issues of data and reporting.

Responsibilities


Ensuring high quality in data and analytics deliverables to uncover opportunities for better media buying and planning
Synthesizing results from research studies, industry intelligence and campaign tracking data into a cohesive story. Use that story to work with media planners and develop recommendations from those results
Building quality, client-facing campaign performance summary presentations in collaboration with agency media planners and buyers.
Collaboration with agency media planners, agency buyers and clients on campaign performance results and data analysis. Orchestration of campaign report building process by communicating expectations, templates and deadlines to other agency team members and setting up check-in meetings.
Presentation of select content from campaign performance summaries and recommendations to client
Designing and executing custom analyses of media performance. Those analyses will include both internal analysis of campaign data executed by the person in this role along with results from external research providers.
Working with research providers to translate study results into media planning recommendations
Performing custom analysis and summarization of media campaign data
Improving automation and reduce human error in data processing and analytic work flows


Required Skills & Experience


Capacity for problem conceptualization and solution design through analytical thinking
Strong interpersonal, written, and verbal communication skills
Strong background in statistical analysis and digital analytics.
Excellent quantitative and analytical skills with the ability to draw conclusions based on data
Comfortable actively participating and contributing in meeting settings with multiple stakeholders
Strong attention to detail, well organized and possesses the ability to prioritize multiple tasks under pressure
Ability to build and deliver impactful, clear and concise presentations
Ability to work collaboratively as part of a cross-functional team
Advanced powerpoint skills to create slide show deliverables for client


Desired Skills & Experience


4+ years of experience in an analytics or performance media function
Bachelor´s degree 
Advance English Level 
Is a highly motivated team player
Experience presenting and interacting with clients
Ability to investigate, analyze and solve problems as well as clearly communicate results through requirement documents
Knowledge of data storage and data management solutions such as Amazon S3 and Snowflake
Knowledge of Tableau data visualization software and site tagging process
Ability to handle multiple projects in a fast-paced environment with the ability to learn and apply new concepts and tools quickly


This role is hybrid, requiring three (3) days per week in the office. The remaining two (2) days may be worked remotely. Specific in-office days will be discussed during the interview process, with flexibility to align with team needs. Please note that the number or required in-office days may be adjusted over time, potentially increasing the number of required in-office days based on business needs.

description:
About the job

Our Purpose

Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.

Title And Summary

Consultant, Advisors & Consulting Services, Marketing

Job Description Summary

Services within Mastercard is responsible for acquiring, engaging, and retaining customers by managing fraud and risk, enhancing cybersecurity, and improving the digital payments experience. We provide value-added services and leverage expertise, data-driven insights, and execution. Our Advisors & Consulting Services team combines traditional management consulting with Mastercard’s rich data assets, proprietary platforms, and technologies to provide clients with powerful strategic insights and recommendations. Our teams work with a diverse global customer base across industries, from banking and payments to retail and restaurants.

The Advisors & Consulting Services group has five specializations: Strategy & Transformation, Performance Analytics, Business Experimentation, Marketing, and Program Management. Our Marketing consultants lead the strategy development and implementation of campaigns and engagements for clients. They use customer data to provide deep direct marketing expertise across channels, evaluating campaign results and adapting the approach to optimize marketing performance.

Positions for different specializations and levels are available in separate job postings. Please review our consulting specializations to learn more about all opportunities and apply for the position that is best suited to your background and experience: https://careers.mastercard.com/us/en/consulting-specializations-at-mastercard

Roles and Responsibilities

Client Impact


 Provide creative input and support deliverables and workstreams on projects across a range of industries and problem statements
 Contribute to the development of marketing strategies and activities for regional and global clients by working with technology and data
 Collaborate with Mastercard team to understand clients’ needs, agenda, and risks
 Develop working relationship with client analysts/managers, and act as trusted and reliable partner



Team Collaboration & Culture


 Collaborate with senior project delivery consultants to identify key findings, prepare effective presentations, and deliver recommendations to clients
 Independently identify issues in defined area of analysis, and structure and synthesize own analysis to highlight relevant findings
 Lead internal and client meetings, and contribute to project management
 Contribute to the firm's intellectual capital
 Receive mentorship from consulting leaders for professional growth and development



Qualifications

Basic qualifications


 Undergraduate degree with work experience focused on one or more of the following fields: marketing content, digital and/or social media campaigns, digital customer experience, digital marketing, offline marketing activities/campaigns, direct response marketing
 Relevant teamwork, and client, internal stakeholder and/or vendor management experience
 Advanced Word, Excel, and PowerPoint skills
 Ability to perform multiple tasks with multiple clients in a fast-paced, deadline-driven environment
 Ability to communicate effectively in English and the local office language (if applicable)
 Eligibility to work in the country where you are applying, as well as apply for travel visas as required by travel needs



Preferred Qualifications


 Additional marketing experience in one or more of the following fields: marketing and campaign experience from agency environment, search engine optimization and search engine marketing, video making (e.g., scripting, storyboard, and content), marketing content, usability labs and experience research, print production management
 Global supply chain management (GSM), including sourcing new suppliers and assessing third party production vendors
 Experience with data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI)
 Experience managing tasks or workstreams in a collaborative team environment with third parties
 Ability to identify problems, brainstorm and analyze answers, and implement the best solutions
 Relevant industry expertise and/or experience in product, sales, or marketing technology



Corporate Security Responsibility

 All activities involving access to Mastercard assets, information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the organization and, therefore, it is expected that every person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is responsible for information security and must:


Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices;
Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed;
Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and
Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.

title: Asesor Financiero Independiente
description:
About the job

Asesor Financiero Independiente | Allianz México




¿Te interesa emprender en el sector financiero y construir un negocio con ingresos ilimitados?

 Buscamos personas con perfil comercial y ganas de desarrollarse como Asesores Financieros Independientes dentro de la promotoría #1 de Allianz en México.




No necesitas experiencia previa en el sector. Nosotros te capacitamos, te entrenamos desde cero y te acompañamos en tus primeras citas con tus clientes para ayudarte a cerrar negocios y acelerar tu crecimiento.




Tu principal responsabilidad

Proporcionar asesorías sobre inversiones, ahorro y planificación del retiro, así como desarrollar estrategias financieras personalizadas que estén alineadas con los objetivos de los clientes.

Desarrollarás y crecerás tu propia cartera de clientes a través de tu red de contactos, recomendaciones y prospección personal.




Lo que ofrecemos

• Capacitación y acompañamiento personalizado desde el inicio.

 • Coaches que te acompañarán a tus primeras citas con tus clientes.

 • Modelo 100% remoto y flexible en toda la República Mexicana.

 • Apoyo económico durante tu capacitación de $45,000 sujeto a resultados.

 • Comisiones altamente competitivas, bonos e ingresos sin tope. Promedio de ingreso mensual variable desde el primer año: ~$50,000 a $70,000 mensuales.

• Viajes, convenciones internacionales y desarrollo profesional continuo.

 • Respaldo de Allianz, una de las compañías financieras más importantes del mundo.




Perfil ideal

• Licenciatura terminada.

 • Red de contactos activa.

 • Habilidades de comunicación y negociación.

 • Mentalidad emprendedora y orientación a resultados.

 • Interés en desarrollar un negocio patrimonial a largo plazo. 

 • Indispensable tener disponibilidad por las mañanas para conectarse a las capacitaciones.




Si buscas independencia, crecimiento profesional y una carrera con alto potencial de ingresos, postúlate para agendar una entrevista.

title: JR Services Member Analyst
skills: You will be supporting our customers under AVEVA Edge applications, leveraging our unique and comprehensive knowledge of AVEVA Edge.

You'll collaborate with Distributors, System Integrators, Certified System Integrators and OEMs for additional support services needed in order to provide their clients with a complete and sophisticated HMI/SCADA solution.
You will also assist clients from all over the world with their HMI/SCADA advanced technical applications through phone calls, e-mails, and remote computer connections.
Additionally, you will develop and customize new product templates.
Bachelor's degree in engineering
Experience with relational databases such as Microsoft SQL Server, Oracle, Sybase or MySQL.
English language proficiency (Advanced)
Spanish language proficiency
Knowledge of HMI/SCADA software, such as AVEVA Edge, InduSoft Web Studio, Wonderware, Intouch, System Platform, OMI, FactoryTalk (OR similar software)
Familiarity with Industrial automation components such as Programmable Logic controllers (PLCs) from manufacturers such as Schneider Electric, Allen-Bradley, Siemens, Omron, Eaton, and Beckhoff.

description:
About the job

AVEVA is a global leader in industrial software. Our cutting-edge solutions are used by thousands of enterprises to deliver the essentials of life – such as energy, infrastructure, chemicals and minerals – safely, efficiently and more sustainably.

We’re the first software business in the world to have our sustainability targets validated by the SBTi, and we’ve been recognized for the transparency and ambition of our commitment to diversity, equity, and inclusion. We’ve also recently been named as one of the world’s most innovative companies.

If you’re a curious and collaborative person who wants to make a big impact through technology, then we want to hear from you! Find out more at AVEVA Careers.

For more information about our privacy policy and how to manage cookies, visit our Privacy Policy.

Location: CDMX

Hybrid: 3x a week - in office

PLEASE apply with a resume in English

Responsibilities - You will do:


You will be supporting our customers under AVEVA Edge applications, leveraging our unique and comprehensive knowledge of AVEVA Edge.
You'll be involved in the support, deployment and maintenance of key client projects.
You'll collaborate with Distributors, System Integrators, Certified System Integrators and OEMs for additional support services needed in order to provide their clients with a complete and sophisticated HMI/SCADA solution.
You will also assist clients from all over the world with their HMI/SCADA advanced technical applications through phone calls, e-mails, and remote computer connections.
Additionally, you will develop and customize new product templates.


Skills & Qualifications - You will have:


Bachelor's degree in engineering
Experience with relational databases such as Microsoft SQL Server, Oracle, Sybase or MySQL.
English language proficiency (Advanced)


A PLUS:


Spanish language proficiency
Knowledge of HMI/SCADA software, such as AVEVA Edge, InduSoft Web Studio, Wonderware, Intouch, System Platform, OMI, FactoryTalk (OR similar software)
Familiarity with Industrial automation components such as Programmable Logic controllers (PLCs) from manufacturers such as Schneider Electric, Allen-Bradley, Siemens, Omron, Eaton, and Beckhoff.
Knowledge of VBScript structured programming language (or similar languages including C, C++, C#, VB, or similar).
Experience with web-based technologies, such as HTML, HTML5, CSS, ActiveX or JavaScript.


AVEVA requires all successful applicants to undergo and pass a comprehensive background check before they start employment. Background checks will be conducted in accordance with local laws and may, subject to those laws, include proof of educational attainment, employment history verification, proof of work authorization, criminal records, identity verification, credit check. Certain positions dealing with sensitive and/or third party personal data may involve additional background check criteria.

AVEVA is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We are committed to being an exemplary employer with an inclusive culture, developing a workplace environment where all our employees are treated with dignity and respect. We value diversity and the expertise that people from different backgrounds bring to our business.

Come and join AVEVA to create the transformative technology that enables our customers to engineer a better world.

description:
About the job

Job Title: QA Engineer (Remote)
Work Mode: Fully Remote
Schedule: Flexible (up to 40 hours per week, based on project availability)




Role Overview:

We are looking for software engineers across Latin America to join a paid remote AI training program. In this role, you will review and evaluate AI-generated code, rate AI responses for accuracy and quality, and write coding challenges to help improve AI model performance. This is a flexible, remote opportunity well-suited for software engineers seeking project-based, asynchronous work. No prior AI experience required.




Key Responsibilities

Review and evaluate code written by AI models for accuracy, efficiency, and best practices
Rate AI responses across key dimensions including correctness, instruction-following, and quality
Write original computer science questions and coding challenges to test AI capabilities
Identify bugs, errors, and inconsistencies in AI-generated code using structured guidelines
Provide clear, constructive, and structured written feedback to improve AI responses
Follow project guidelines, quality standards, and confidentiality requirements




Qualifications

Software engineering experience — junior, mid-level, or senior welcome
Proficiency in one or more of the following: Python, Java, JavaScript, TypeScript, C++, Swift, or Rust
Strong attention to detail, analytical thinking, and problem-solving skills
Background in full stack, backend, frontend, mobile, or any engineering discipline




Eligibility Requirements

Candidates must be legally authorised to work in one of the following locations:
Argentina, Bolivia, Brazil, Belize, Chile, Colombia, Costa Rica, Dominican Republic, Dominica, Ecuador, El Salvador, Guatemala, Honduras, Mexico, Nicaragua, Panama, Paraguay, Peru, Puerto Rico, Trinidad and Tobago, Uruguay, Venezuela




Compensation

Earn up to $34 USD/hr — core project rate with additional performance incentives averaging 7.5% on top
Payments issued weekly via supported payout platforms (e.g., PayPal or AirTM)




Work Arrangement

Fully remote and asynchronous
Flexible workload depending on project availability
Free access to leading AI tools worth $200+/month included




Equal Opportunity Statement Selection decisions are based solely on skills, qualifications, and project requirements. We are committed to inclusive and fair engagement practices and consider all qualified applicants without regard to legally protected characteristics.




Ready to put your coding skills to work on AI? Apply now — interviews begin within days of application.

description:
About the job

Our Purpose

Mastercard powers economies and empowers people in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with our customers, we’re helping build a sustainable economy where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of digital payments choices, making transactions secure, simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation, partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of products and services that help people, businesses and governments realize their greatest potential.

Title And Summary

Research Consultant, Advisors Research (CDMX)

Research Consultant, Advisors Research Center, Mexico (CDMX)

Our Purpose

We work to connect and power an inclusive, digital economy that benefits everyone, everywhere by making transactions safe, simple, smart, and accessible. Using secure data and networks, partnerships and passion, our innovations and solutions help individuals, financial institutions, governments, and businesses realize their greatest potential. Our decency quotient, or DQ, drives our culture and everything we do inside and outside of our company. We cultivate a culture of inclusion for all employees that respects their individual strengths, views, and experiences. We believe that our differences enable us to be a better team – one that makes better decisions, drives innovation, and delivers better business results.

Job Title

Research Consultant, Advisors Research Center

All About Us

The Data & Services team is a key differentiator for Mastercard providing the cutting-edge services that help our customers grow. Focused on thinking big and scaling fast around the globe, this agile team is responsible for end-to-end solutions for a diverse global customer base. We combine traditional management consulting with our rich data assets and in-house technology to provide our clients with powerful strategic insights and recommendations. Centered on data-driven technologies and innovation, these services include consulting, loyalty and marketing programs, test-and-learn business experimentation, and data driven information and risk management services. While specializing in the payments industry, Mastercard Data & Services also works closely with major retailers, airlines, and other enterprises, leveraging data and insights garnered from within and beyond its network.

All About The Role

Advisors Research Center is a new capability designed to respond to fast evolving customer needs for high-quality research support, with the important mission of value realization in developing insights and recommendations. This capability augments Advisors traditional areas of strength.

In this role, we are looking for someone who can confidently oversee and support research projects from start to finish, collaborate seamlessly with clients and vendors, and deliver high-quality, actionable insights through thorough data analysis and reporting.

Your Responsibilities Would Include:


 Support research projects, including primary and secondary methodologies, while ensuring timelines, budgets, and quality standards are met.
 Aggregate and analyze data to deliver clear, actionable insights through client-ready reports and presentations.
 Collaborate with clients to understand their needs and provide tailored solutions, while effectively managing vendor relationships for timely and accurate project delivery.
 Ensure the preparation and delivery of high-quality research materials with a focus on accuracy and consistency.



All About You


 2 – 4 years of experience involved end-to-end research projects, including primary research (focus group discussions, in-depth interviews, survey development and analysis, and quantitative techniques).
 Experience managing and collaborating with third-party research vendors, maintaining an ongoing understanding of current issues, and proposing and taking appropriate action.
 Ability to synthesize and analyze information to deliver meaningful conclusions and actionable recommendations.
 Ability to prepare insightful presentations by aggregating data from various sources.
 Strong business writing skills, with the ability to create client-ready materials.
 Proficient in coordinating, preparing, and ensuring the timely delivery and accuracy of research projects, materials, and reports.



All About Your Education & Skills


 Proficiency in English is a must.
 Advanced skills in Word, Excel, PowerPoint are essential; familiarity with project management tools is preferred.
 Prior experience working in a research agency is strongly preferred.
 Experience in the financial sector is desirable.



Corporate Security Responsibility

All Activities Involving Access To Mastercard Assets, Information, And Networks Comes With An Inherent Risk To The Organization And, Therefore, It Is Expected That Every Person Working For, Or On Behalf Of, Mastercard Is Responsible For Information Security And Must:


 Abide by Mastercard’s security policies and practices.
 Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information being accessed.
 Report any suspected information security violation or breach, and
 Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines

Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in accordance with Mastercard’s guidelines.

title: Front Desk Associate
skills: Create a welcoming community environment for our members and guests (and sometimes their pets!) by greeting all who enter our spaces warmly.
Make members and guests feel known and cared for by proactively learning their names, answering their questions, and addressing their needs or concerns.
Support the Community Management team with maintaining the front desk operations throughout the day including mail and package handling.
Help ensure your building is fully operational and make adjustments when needed.
Take direction from the Community Associate, Community Lead and the Community Manager to support the Community Team as necessary.
Be an expert in WeWork products and services and appropriately promote WeWork-provided service offerings to our members and guests.
All of this while illustrating WeWork’s core values and working towards achieving our mission.
Greet all members and guests with a warm and welcoming demeanor.
Cover the front desk during the building’s set business hours and be an on-site point of contact for members, located at the Community Bar.
Ensure visiting guests are registered and checked in and in accordance with WeWork’s guest policies.
Provide orientation of our spaces and relevant information to ensure all first-time visitors and guests feel welcome and supported.
Support with issuing and collect keys from members and guests as needed.
Keep the front desk clean, organized and free of clutter at all times.
Notify members of any food deliveries and packages.
Learn the names of members and guests and get to know their businesses with the goal of building relationships in order to facilitate the sense of Community that WeWork is known for.
Keep track of relevant member notes and information in WeWork systems.
Answer questions from members and guests knowledgeably and be able to offer appropriate WeWork products and solutions to support where relevant.
Identify opportunities to celebrate members’ successes and milestones.
Know and be able to explain WeWork policies and procedures to members and guests.
Know and recommend local restaurants, food delivery services, catering options, team outing venues, post office, shipping center, supply store, etc.
Assist with the handling mail and packages throughout the day including receiving, processing, sorting and organizing.
Know and be able to explain WeWork policies and procedures around mail and mailroom.
Monitor our spaces and make adjustments when needed, for example, picking up rubbish, resetting chairs, and straightening pillows.
Help maintain building amenities throughout the day, for example, restocking of the Community treat bowl, keycards, printers, etc.
Respond to emergencies and incidents promptly and appropriately, escalating and recording as needed.
Bachelors Degree in Tourism, Hospitality or related
Proficient in English
Availability to work onsite in Avenida de Las Americas, Guadalajara
+1 year experience as a Front Desk
You’re a people-person—you enjoy meeting new people from all walks of life
In-person interactions energize you and you have outstanding interpersonal skills
You feel comfortable and confident being the face of the brand
Helping others gives you a sense of accomplishment
You work well in a team and can roll up your sleeves to help when needed
You’re able to multitask while always prioritizing the member experience
You’ve worked in hospitality / retail or customer service before which is preferred but not required
You have strong verbal and written communication skills
You are comfortable with and proficient in basic computer skills
You demonstrate and value integrity, dependability, responsibility, accountability, self-awareness, work ethic, and compassion
description:
About the job

About Us

At WeWork, we provide flexible workspace solutions to help businesses of all sizes thrive in more than 37 countries worldwide. The future of work is here, and we’re not just a part of that movement—we’re leading it. United by a desire to build a global community, our purpose is to give members the support to do their best work, and the space to take their ideas to new heights. Join us as we empower tomorrow’s world at work.

About The Opportunity

As a Front Desk Associate, you’ll be the heart and “face” of WeWork’s hospitality team as the primary point of contact for our members and guests! While no one day will look exactly the same, you will report to one of WeWork’s beautiful locations on weekdays only to support the Community Management team.

Depending on the location you are assigned, you’ll either work with a variety of members from different backgrounds and industries, or focus your efforts on one of our Enterprise clients. Employees supporting our Enterprise locations may have different holidays, technical systems, operational and relationship requirements, and property management responsibilities than employees in our Classic locations.

Duties

Your work, which will include but not be limited to the duties listed below, will help WeWork to achieve the following:


Create a welcoming community environment for our members and guests (and sometimes their pets!) by greeting all who enter our spaces warmly.
Make members and guests feel known and cared for by proactively learning their names, answering their questions, and addressing their needs or concerns.
Support the Community Management team with maintaining the front desk operations throughout the day including mail and package handling. 
Help ensure your building is fully operational and make adjustments when needed. 
Take direction from the Community Associate, Community Lead and the Community Manager to support the Community Team as necessary.
Be an expert in WeWork products and services and appropriately promote WeWork-provided service offerings to our members and guests. 
All of this while illustrating WeWork’s core values and working towards achieving our mission.


In this role, you’ll be responsible for the following areas: 

Front Desk Management


Greet all members and guests with a warm and welcoming demeanor.
Cover the front desk during the building’s set business hours and be an on-site point of contact for members, located at the Community Bar.
Ensure visiting guests are registered and checked in and in accordance with WeWork’s guest policies.
Provide orientation of our spaces and relevant information to ensure all first-time visitors and guests feel welcome and supported.
Support with issuing and collect keys from members and guests as needed.
Keep the front desk clean, organized and free of clutter at all times.
Notify members of any food deliveries and packages.


Member Engagement


Learn the names of members and guests and get to know their businesses with the goal of building relationships in order to facilitate the sense of Community that WeWork is known for.
Keep track of relevant member notes and information in WeWork systems.
Answer questions from members and guests knowledgeably and be able to offer appropriate WeWork products and solutions to support where relevant.
Identify opportunities to celebrate members’ successes and milestones.
Know and be able to explain WeWork policies and procedures to members and guests.
Know and recommend local restaurants, food delivery services, catering options, team outing venues, post office, shipping center, supply store, etc.


Building Operations and Management


Assist with the handling mail and packages throughout the day including receiving, processing, sorting and organizing.
Know and be able to explain WeWork policies and procedures around mail and mailroom.
Monitor our spaces and make adjustments when needed, for example, picking up rubbish, resetting chairs, and straightening pillows.
Help maintain building amenities throughout the day, for example, restocking of the Community treat bowl, keycards, printers, etc.
Respond to emergencies and incidents promptly and appropriately, escalating and recording as needed.


About You


Bachelors Degree in Tourism, Hospitality or related
Proficient in English
Availability to work onsite in Avenida de Las Americas, Guadalajara
+1 year experience as a Front Desk
You’re a people-person—you enjoy meeting new people from all walks of life
In-person interactions energize you and you have outstanding interpersonal skills
You feel comfortable and confident being the face of the brand
Helping others gives you a sense of accomplishment
You work well in a team and can roll up your sleeves to help when needed
You’re able to multitask while always prioritizing the member experience
You’ve worked in hospitality / retail or customer service before which is preferred but not required
You have strong verbal and written communication skills
You are comfortable with and proficient in basic computer skills
You demonstrate and value integrity, dependability, responsibility, accountability, self-awareness, work ethic, and compassion


Life at WeWork

Being a WeWorker is more than just a job. We believe the magic of work is sparked by the passion you bring, the places you go, the people you meet and the purpose you follow. And it starts here. Here you will brush shoulders with those who dare to dream and do. Here you will be welcomed by a community that embraces and inspires you—because together we can achieve more. Here we challenge ideas, and explore new ways of getting things done. Whether you are part of our Employee Community Groups, or part of a global project, we ask you to bring your open-minded attitude and collaborative spirit. In return, you will be part of a team where your unique perspectives are celebrated.

About the job

Role Overview:

We are hiring for one of our clients, seeking an Engineering & Technical Documentation Specialist to work on a contract basis. The role involves creating, maintaining, and updating technical documentation for software, APIs, and infrastructure components. You will collaborate with engineering teams to ensure documentation aligns with product releases and architectural changes.




Key Responsibilities:

• Develop and maintain clear, concise, and accurate technical documentation for software products and services.

• Collaborate with engineers, product managers, and support teams to gather and refine technical content.

• Ensure documentation is up-to-date with the latest product releases, API changes, and system updates.

• Write and edit user guides, API references, installation instructions, and troubleshooting materials.

• Review and validate technical content for accuracy, consistency, and adherence to style guidelines.




Required Skills & Qualifications:

• Experience writing technical documentation for software, APIs, or infrastructure systems is required.

• Proficiency in Markdown, reStructuredText, or other markup languages for documentation is required.

• Familiarity with version control systems like Git for documentation management is preferred.

• Strong understanding of software development lifecycles and agile methodologies is preferred.

• Excellent written communication skills with the ability to simplify complex technical concepts is required.




More About the Opportunity:

This role offers a unique opportunity to work with a global leader in the Technology, Information and Internet industry, contributing to the scalability and usability of technical resources. The role is ideal for professionals who thrive in a remote, collaborative environment.




Equal Opportunity Employer:

We hire based on skills and expertise. All qualified candidates are welcome regardless of background, experience, or prior employment history. Applications are reviewed solely on demonstrated technical ability and qualifications.
